content:
<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/skills/yolo-ship

# yolo-ship — implement a task end-to-end (autonomously)

## Overview

One task → one worktree → four phases (**brainstorm → design → implement → ship**). The heavy work (codebase reads, per-task implementation) is pushed to **subagents** so your own context stays lean. You make and **document** decisions instead of asking the user. You are not done until the PR is **CI-green and review-clean** — you wait for CodeRabbit, address it, and fix failing tests in a loop.

This skill orchestrates other skills. It does not re-explain them — it sequences them and adds the autonomy contract + the ship loop.

## The autonomy contract (hard rules)

1. **Don't ask the user.** Make a recommendation and proceed. Escalate via `AskUserQuestion` ONLY when a decision is high-stakes **and** ambiguous **and** not inferable from code, memory, or conventions. "I want to be safe" is not a reason to ask — it's a reason to document.
2. **Every non-trivial decision is logged** to `.claude/memory/decisions.md` (Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives). If you'd have asked the user, write the recommendation there instead.
3. **Follow-up work goes in `TODO.md`** — never silently dropped. Anything you deliberately defer is a TODO line, not a memory.
4. **Pre-PR gate is `pnpm build` + `pnpm test` + lint** — not just build+test (see [[feedback_run_lint_before_pr]], [[feedback_run_tsc_alongside_vitest]]).
5. **Done = CI green + CodeRabbit comments addressed + no new actionable comments.** You do **NOT** merge. Stop at a green, review-clean PR and report the link.

## Context budget (target < 300–400K tokens)

The orchestrator (you) holds only: the plan file, project memory, and one-paragraph summaries from each subagent. Never the raw file dumps.

- **Codebase exploration** → dispatch the `Explore` subagent. It returns conclusions, not file contents.
- **Per-task implementation** → dispatch one subagent per plan task (subagent-driven-development). Each returns a short summary of what it changed + test status. ~50–100× context savings vs. doing it inline.
- If your context climbs past ~250K, flush state to the plan file + memory and keep leaning on them. Don't try to hold everything.

## The phases

### Phase 0 — Isolate
- **REQUIRED:** Use superpowers:using-git-worktrees (or the `EnterWorktree` tool) to create a fresh worktree + branch for this task. All work happens there.
- **REQUIRED:** Use claude-memory — read `.claude/memory/` so prior decisions/mistakes/patterns inform the work.

### Phase 1 — Brainstorm (run it autonomously)
- **REQUIRED:** Use superpowers:brainstorming — but in self-answering mode. Generate the questions it would ask the user, then answer each one yourself from the codebase, `.claude/memory/`, CLAUDE.md, and architecture docs. Log each material answer to `decisions.md`.
- Use `Explore` subagents for any "how does X currently work?" question so the exploration doesn't bloat your context.
- Output: a tight problem statement + chosen approach (a few paragraphs), not a transcript.

### Phase 2 — Design
- **REQUIRED:** Use superpowers:writing-plans — produce a written plan broken into **independent, testable tasks**. Save it to a file (e.g. `docs/plans/<date>-<slug>.md` or the worktree root).
- **REQUIRED for AX code:** Use ax-conventions — honor the six invariants; do the boundary-review checklist for any new/changed hook.
- Apply a YAGNI pass ([[feedback_yagni_check_in_plans]]): mark each task "load-bearing at MVP or dead code?" — cut the dead.
- If the task touches a sandbox boundary, IPC, plugin loading, untrusted content, or new dependencies, note that Phase 3 must run security-checklist.

### Phase 3 — Implement (subagent-driven)
- **REQUIRED:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development — dispatch one subagent per plan task. Each subagent uses superpowers:test-driven-development (test first) and returns a summary.
- After each task, review the returned diff against the plan (superpowers:requesting-code-review / receiving-code-review). Don't rubber-stamp; verify claims.
- New hook surface or sensitive boundary touched → run security-checklist before moving on.

### Phase 4 — Pre-PR gate
- **REQUIRED:** Use superpowers:verification-before-completion — run the real commands, read the real output. Evidence before claims.
- Run `pnpm build && pnpm test` (or `--filter @ax/<plugin>`) **and** lint. tsc must be clean, not just vitest ([[feedback_run_tsc_alongside_vitest]]).
- Do a **whole-branch** review, not just per-task — a shared-table FK or repo-wide teardown break only shows on the full build ([[feedback_new_fk_breaks_downstream_test_teardown]]).
- Write every deferred item into `TODO.md`.

### Phase 5 — Ship: PR + CodeRabbit + CI loop
This is where you must not stop early. Follow the loop below until both conditions are green.

```dot
digraph ship {
    "Open PR" [shape=box];
    "CodeRabbit done?" [shape=diamond];
    "Wait (poll)" [shape=box];
    "Actionable comments?" [shape=diamond];
    "Address + push follow-up commit" [shape=box];
    "CI green?" [shape=diamond];
    "Fix failing tests + push" [shape=box];
    "Done: report PR link (do NOT merge)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Open PR" -> "CodeRabbit done?";
    "CodeRabbit done?" -> "Wait (poll)" [label="no"];
    "Wait (poll)" -> "CodeRabbit done?";
    "CodeRabbit done?" -> "Actionable comments?" [label="yes"];
    "Actionable comments?" -> "Address + push follow-up commit" [label="yes"];
    "Address + push follow-up commit" -> "CI green?";
    "Actionable comments?" -> "CI green?" [label="no"];
    "CI green?" -> "Fix failing tests + push" [label="no"];
    "Fix failing tests + push" -> "CodeRabbit done?";
    "CI green?" -> "Done: report PR link (do NOT merge)" [label="yes"];
}
```

- **Open the PR against `main`:** use commit-commands:commit-push-pr (or superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch → PR option). **CodeRabbit only reviews PRs whose base is `main`** — target any other branch and it never reviews. Pass `--base main` explicitly; don't stack onto a feature branch. Boundary review answers belong in the PR body if hooks changed.
- **Wait for CodeRabbit** (`coderabbitai[bot]`). It first posts a "Currently reviewing…" placeholder, then edits in the real review (usually a few minutes). Detect completion:
  ```bash
  gh pr view <n> --json reviews,comments \
    --jq '[.reviews[],.comments[]] | map(select(.author.login=="coderabbitai")) | last | .body' \
    | grep -qiv "currently reviewing\|review in progress"
  ```
  While waiting, do **not** busy-spin in context. Either poll with short sleeps (~270s, keeps the prompt cache warm) or use `ScheduleWakeup` (~600s+) and let the run resume.
- **Mind the review budget.** CodeRabbit rate-limits reviews (~3–5 per hour, refilling over time) and **re-reviews on every push** — each push spends one review. The remaining count + refill ETA appear at the bottom of every review walkthrough (e.g. "2/5 reviews remaining, refill in 42 minutes"). Check quota **without** spending a review by commenting `@coderabbitai reviews remaining?` (or `@coderabbitai rate limit`). Read the budget before you push.
- **Batch fixes; push once per cycle.** Address *all* outstanding CodeRabbit comments **and** all CI/test failures, commit them granularly (targeted follow-up commits, not amend — [[feedback_targeted_followup_commits]]), then push the batch in a single shot so CodeRabbit consumes one review, not one per comment. If the budget is exhausted, `ScheduleWakeup` for the stated refill window and push then — pushing into a rate-limited void just skips the review.
- **Address comments** with receiving-code-review discipline — verify each suggestion; push back in the PR thread on ones that are wrong, fix the rest. Minor + "ready to merge" means ship ([[feedback_minor_issues_non_blocking]]).
- **CI:** `gh pr checks <n>`. On red, use superpowers:systematic-debugging — fix the root cause, add a regression test (Bug Fix Policy). Fold the fix into the same batched push above.
- After each batched push, re-check CodeRabbit (it re-reviews the new commits) and CI. **Exit only when CI is green AND no unaddressed actionable CodeRabbit comments remain.** Then report the PR link and stop.

## Red flags — you are rationalizing

| Thought | Reality |
|---|---|
| "I'll ask the user to be safe" | Document a recommendation in `decisions.md` and proceed. Asking is the exception, not the default. |
| "I'll skip lint, build+test passed" | The gate is build+test+**lint**. tsc/lint catch what vitest tolerates. |
| "I'll defer this but it's obvious" | Obvious-to-you ≠ tracked. Put it in `TODO.md` or it's lost. |
| "CI will probably pass, I'll wrap up" | Not done until `gh pr checks` is actually green. Verify, don't assume. |
| "CodeRabbit is slow, I'll skip waiting" | Waiting for the review is the task. Poll/Schedule and come back. |
| "I'll push each fix as I make it" | CodeRabbit re-reviews every push and caps at ~3–5/hr. Batch fixes, push once per cycle, check `@coderabbitai reviews remaining?` first. |
| "I'll base this PR on my other branch" | CodeRabbit only reviews PRs against `main`. Any other base = no review. |
| "I'll implement inline, subagents are overhead" | Inline implementation blows the context budget. Dispatch per task. |
| "This decision is too small to log" | If you'd have asked the user about it, it's big enough to log. |

## Quick reference — what this orchestrates

| Phase | Skill / tool |
|---|---|
| Isolate | superpowers:using-git-worktrees, `EnterWorktree`, claude-memory |
| Brainstorm | superpowers:brainstorming, `Explore` subagent |
| Design | superpowers:writing-plans, ax-conventions, security-checklist |
| Implement | superpowers:subagent-driven-development, superpowers:test-driven-development |
| Verify | superpowers:verification-before-completion, superpowers:requesting-code-review |
| Ship | commit-commands:commit-push-pr, superpowers:receiving-code-review, superpowers:systematic-debugging, `gh`, `ScheduleWakeup` |


ARGUMENTS: Read and execute .claude/prompts/implement-f1-f2-resync-followups.md

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- assistant-ui: Guide for assistant-ui library - AI chat UI components. Use when asking about architecture, debugging, or understanding the codebase.
- thread-list: Guide for multi-thread management in assistant-ui. Use when implementing thread lists, switching threads, or managing conversation history.
- update: Update assistant-ui and AI SDK to latest versions. Detects current versions, identifies breaking changes, and executes migrations.
- runtime: Guide for assistant-ui runtime system and state management. Use when working with runtimes, accessing state, or managing thread/message data.
- setup: Setup and configure assistant-ui in a project. Use when installing packages, configuring runtimes, setting up chat UI, or troubleshooting setup issues.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- streaming: Guide for assistant-stream package and streaming protocols. Use when implementing streaming backends, custom protocols, or debugging stream issues.
- primitives: Guide for assistant-ui UI primitives - ThreadPrimitive, ComposerPrimitive, MessagePrimitive. Use when customizing chat UI components.
- security-checklist: Use when adding or modifying — sandbox boundaries, IPC handlers, plugin loading or manifests, hook payloads carrying model/tool/external-system output, dependencies in package.json, or code that uses caller-provided file paths / spawns processes / opens network connections. Walks three threat models (sandbox escape, prompt injection, supply chain) and produces a structured PR security note. Rigid checklist — every item must be answered, "N/A" requires a reason.
- claude-memory: Use at session start to read project memory in `.claude/memory/`, during the session to capture corrections / dead ends / "remember this" / "log that", and at session end ("done", "wrap up", PR opened, tests pass) to update it. Five files — context.md, decisions.md, patterns.md, mistakes.md, meta.md — keep project facts, decisions, reusable patterns, mistakes to avoid, and self-observations about how I work on this project. Also handles first-session bootstrap (creating the directory, seeding files, gitignoring).
- chat-qa-sweep: Use when you want to QA / pressure-test / smoke-test / regression-sweep the chat UI against the local kind cluster ax-next-dev and get a findings report — not fix one bug, but run a fixed battery of common activities (new chat, npx, skills, attachments, artifacts, reload old sessions, title generation, parallel sessions) plus fault injection (sandbox killed mid-turn, host killed, provider error, network blip) and report what broke or glitched. Triggers on "pressure test the chat", "QA the chat UI", "run a chat smoke sweep", "chaos-test chat", "find UI glitches", "make sure chat still works end to end".
- yolo-ship: Use when asked to implement, build, or ship a task or feature end-to-end with minimal supervision and you are expected to own it from request to a passing, review-clean pull request. Triggers on "implement X end to end", "build X and open a PR", "take this and run with it autonomously", "ship this task", or any request to deliver a unit of work with minimal back-and-forth.
- shadcn: Manages shadcn components and projects — adding, searching, fixing, debugging, styling, and composing UI. Provides project context, component docs, and usage examples. Applies when working with shadcn/ui, component registries, presets, --preset codes, or any project with a components.json file. Also triggers for "shadcn init", "create an app with --preset", or "switch to --preset". **Monorepo:** ax-next is a pnpm monorepo and the shadcn workspace lives in `packages/channel-web` — every shadcn CLI call must include `-c packages/channel-web` or the CLI errors with `monorepo_root`.
- ax-conventions: Use when writing or modifying any AX plugin or hook — covers the five invariants (transport/storage-agnostic hooks, no cross-plugin imports, no half-wired plugins, one source of truth, capabilities explicit and minimized), the plugin manifest format, hook bus mechanics, the boundary review checklist, and common patterns
- k8s-acceptance-loop: Use when the user wants to verify expected behavior in the browser UI against the local kind cluster `ax-next-dev` and iterate until it works. The verification mechanism is Playwright MCP — drive the chat UI (or whatever surface the user names) in a real browser, observe outcomes (console, network, DOM snapshot), and loop on failures. Picks between an image-rebuild loop (Dockerfile / chart / env / runner-side code) and a fast hostPath-mounted dist loop (host-side TypeScript only). Triggers on phrases like "get this working in the browser against ax-next-dev", "loop until the chat sends a response", "fix until the UI shows X", "use playwright to verify Y in the cluster", or any request to keep fixing until a browser-observable outcome passes. Works for scripted procedures in `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md`, ad-hoc bug repros, or any scenario the user describes in UI terms.
- background: Run a command or agent in the background (non-blocking)
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- pr-review-toolkit:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs quality report, then makes targeted updates. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the handoff prompt file that defines this task.

content.content:
1	# Implement F-1 and F-2: complete the concurrent-writer re-sync fix
2	
3	## What this is
4	
5	PR #132 (`fix/workspace-concurrent-write-turn-loss`) fixed the bug where a concurrent writer
6	(`attachments:commit` → `workspace:apply`) advances the shared per-(userId,agentId) workspace
7	git mirror out-of-band, causing the chat runner's `commit-notify` to stuck-loop and lose
8	turns. That fix is **server-backend-specific and per-turn only**. Two documented follow-ups
9	remain — implement BOTH in this session.
10	
11	**Read first for full context:**
12	- `docs/plans/2026-05-23-chat-transcript-loss-fix-impl.md` — the fix plan + root cause + the
13	  "Follow-ups surfaced by the final whole-implementation review" section (F-1, F-2).
14	- The PR #132 diff (the re-sync mechanism you'll be extending): host returns
15	  `{accepted:false, actualParent, baselineBundleBytes}` on a version mismatch; the runner
16	  re-syncs via `resyncBaselineAndReplay` (`git rebase --onto`) + a bounded retry loop.
17	
18	## Setup
19	
20	1. **Base off latest `main`** — it must already contain PR #132's re-sync code. Verify:
21	   `git -C <repo> grep -l resyncBaselineAndReplay origin/main` should hit
22	   `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/git-workspace.ts`, and
23	   `git grep "baselineBundleBytes" origin/main -- packages/ipc-protocol` should hit
24	   `actions.ts`. If those aren't on main yet, PR #132 hasn't merged — base off
25	   `origin/fix/workspace-concurrent-write-turn-loss` instead (and note it in the PR).
26	2. **Work in an isolated git worktree** (`EnterWorktree`, or `git worktree add`). This repo's
27	   single working tree is shared with concurrent background agents that switch branches
28	   mid-task — do NOT commit on a branch in the shared checkout. Run `pnpm install` in the
29	   worktree.
30	3. Follow `CLAUDE.md`: TDD, the bug-fix-test policy ("a bug fix that wasn't caught by an
31	   existing test MUST add a test that would have caught it"), the six invariants (esp. **no
32	   cross-plugin imports** and **capabilities minimized**), and the shadcn/UI rules (N/A here).
33	4. Invoke the `security-checklist` skill for F-1 (it touches the `commit-notify` IPC handler,
34	   which processes runner-supplied bundles across the sandbox→host trust boundary).
35	
36	---
37	
38	## F-1 — make re-sync work on the single-replica `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) backend
39	
40	**Problem.** The re-sync signal currently only fires from the `commit-notify` handler's
41	**export-baseline-bundle** catch (added in PR #132,
42	`packages/ipc-core/src/handlers/workspace-commit-notify.ts` ~lines 150-160 — it forwards
43	`actualParent` + `baselineBundleBytes`). But the single-replica backend
44	`packages/workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts` **deliberately does not strict-check HEAD on
45	export** (see the comment ~line 248 — it bundles a stale-but-reachable OID), so on that
46	backend the version mismatch does NOT surface at export. It surfaces later at **apply-bundle**
47	(impl.ts raises `code:'parent-mismatch'` at ~lines 793/1027/1047). The handler's **apply-bundle
48	parent-mismatch catch** returns a **bare** `{accepted:false, reason}` with no
49	`actualParent`/`baselineBundleBytes` — so the runner treats it as a true veto and rolls back.
50	Net: on the single-replica backend, the original turn-loss bug is **unfixed**.
51	
52	**Fix.** In `workspace-commit-notify.ts`, the apply-bundle `parent-mismatch` catch must
53	forward the same re-sync envelope as the export-baseline-bundle catch:
54	1. Confirm the apply-bundle `parent-mismatch` `PluginError` carries `cause.actualParent` on
55	   BOTH backends (`workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts` and `workspace-git-server/src/client/git-engine.ts`
56	   — the `parentMismatch` helper). If `workspace-git-core` doesn't set `cause.actualParent`,
57	   add it (it's the freshly-read mirror/storage HEAD).
58	2. In the catch, when `actualParent` is present, obtain `baselineBundleBytes` **at**
59	   `actualParent` by calling the `workspace:export-baseline-bundle` service hook with
60	   `{version: actualParent}` (since `actualParent` IS the current head, the export succeeds
61	   and returns the bundle at that head — this is exactly what the runner needs to rebase
62	   onto). Do NOT import a backend directly — go through the hook (invariant: no cross-plugin
63	   imports).
64	3. Return `{accepted:false, actualParent, baselineBundleBytes}` (schema-validated via
65	   `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema`; `baselineBundleBytes` already validates as canonical
66	   base64). Other apply-bundle errors still behave as before.
67	
68	The runner side needs **no change** — its existing re-sync loop already consumes this envelope.
69	
70	**Regression test (must fail pre-fix).** Add an integration test that drives the
71	`workspace-git-core` (single-replica) backend through the production sequence: materialize →
72	turn-1 commit-notify (V1) → a concurrent out-of-band `workspace:apply` advancing to V2 (disjoint
73	path) → turn-2 `commit-notify` with `parent=V1` → assert the response is
74	`{accepted:false, actualParent:V2, baselineBundleBytes:<non-empty>}` (NOT a bare veto). Mirror
75	the existing server-backend regression at
76	`packages/workspace-git-server/src/client/__tests__/concurrent-attachment-regression.test.ts`,
77	but exercise the apply-bundle path on workspace-git-core. Use REAL components (no mocks).
78	
79	---
80	
81	## F-2 — re-sync the runner's final/idle commit too (not just per-turn)
82	
83	**Problem.** `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts` has the bounded re-sync + retry
84	loop in the **per-turn** `result` handler (~lines 1006-1095). But the **final commit** site
85	(~lines 1182-1212 — the post-`result` commit that captures the SDK's delayed jsonl write, per
86	PR #127) only logs on `!accepted`; it has **no re-sync**. A concurrent writer racing that
87	final commit drops the final turn's tail — the same bug class, narrower window.
88	
89	**Fix.** Extract the per-turn bounded re-sync loop into a single shared helper in the runner
90	(e.g. `commitNotifyWithResync({ client, root, bundleBytes, parentVersion, reason }):
91	Promise<{ parentVersion: string | null; outcome: 'accepted'|'rolled-back'|'kept' }>`), and call
92	it at BOTH sites: the per-turn `result` handler AND the final commit site. Preserve all current
93	behavior exactly (accept → advance baseline + update parentVersion; concurrent-writer envelope
94	→ `resyncBaselineAndReplay` + bounded retry, `MAX_RESYNC_ATTEMPTS`; true veto → rollback;
95	network/5xx → keep tree, accumulate; `reb===null` after resync → promote parentVersion). The
96	helper must keep the result handler thin and must NOT alter `chat:turn-end` emission.
97	
98	**Regression test (must fail pre-fix).** Extend `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/__tests__/main.test.ts`:
99	drive a session where the **final** commit-notify (after the turn loop drains) returns the
100	concurrent-writer envelope, and assert the runner re-syncs + retries (final tail persists)
101	rather than just logging. Reuse the existing fake-client/queryMock harness from the
102	`concurrent-writer advance` tests in that file.
103	
104	---
105	
106	## Definition of done
107	
108	- Both F-1 and F-2 implemented, each with a regression test that is RED before the fix.
109	- `pnpm build` (whole repo) + `pnpm test` (at least the changed packages: `@ax/ipc-core`,
110	  `@ax/workspace-git-core`, `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`, `@ax/ipc-protocol`; ideally
111	  whole-repo) green; `pnpm exec eslint <changed files>` clean (scope to changed files — a
112	  whole-repo `pnpm lint` exits 1 on stale worktree dirs that aren't yours).
113	- Update `docs/plans/2026-05-23-chat-transcript-loss-fix-impl.md` §Follow-ups to mark F-1/F-2
114	  done.
115	- Open a PR (CodeRabbit auto-reviews PRs based on main; it rate-limits, so batch fixes).
116	- The shared per-(userId,agentId) mirror + concurrent-writer model is the crux — if anything
117	  about the topology is unclear, re-read the root-cause section of the plan doc and the PR #132
118	  diff before guessing.
119	
120	## Reuse, don't reinvent
121	- `parentMismatch` helper + `cause.actualParent` contract — `workspace-git-server/src/client/git-engine.ts`.
122	- `BundleBytesSchema` (canonical-base64) + the `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponse` envelope — `packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts`.
123	- `resyncBaselineAndReplay` + `advanceBaseline`/`rollbackToBaseline`/`commitTurnAndBundle` — `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/git-workspace.ts`.
124	- The export-baseline-bundle parent-mismatch catch (PR #132) is the template for F-1's apply-bundle catch — `workspace-commit-notify.ts` ~150-160.

file.content:
# Implement F-1 and F-2: complete the concurrent-writer re-sync fix

## What this is

PR #132 (`fix/workspace-concurrent-write-turn-loss`) fixed the bug where a concurrent writer
(`attachments:commit` → `workspace:apply`) advances the shared per-(userId,agentId) workspace
git mirror out-of-band, causing the chat runner's `commit-notify` to stuck-loop and lose
turns. That fix is **server-backend-specific and per-turn only**. Two documented follow-ups
remain — implement BOTH in this session.

**Read first for full context:**
- `docs/plans/2026-05-23-chat-transcript-loss-fix-impl.md` — the fix plan + root cause + the
  "Follow-ups surfaced by the final whole-implementation review" section (F-1, F-2).
- The PR #132 diff (the re-sync mechanism you'll be extending): host returns
  `{accepted:false, actualParent, baselineBundleBytes}` on a version mismatch; the runner
  re-syncs via `resyncBaselineAndReplay` (`git rebase --onto`) + a bounded retry loop.

## Setup

1. **Base off latest `main`** — it must already contain PR #132's re-sync code. Verify:
   `git -C <repo> grep -l resyncBaselineAndReplay origin/main` should hit
   `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/git-workspace.ts`, and
   `git grep "baselineBundleBytes" origin/main -- packages/ipc-protocol` should hit
   `actions.ts`. If those aren't on main yet, PR #132 hasn't merged — base off
   `origin/fix/workspace-concurrent-write-turn-loss` instead (and note it in the PR).
2. **Work in an isolated git worktree** (`EnterWorktree`, or `git worktree add`). This repo's
   single working tree is shared with concurrent background agents that switch branches
   mid-task — do NOT commit on a branch in the shared checkout. Run `pnpm install` in the
   worktree.
3. Follow `CLAUDE.md`: TDD, the bug-fix-test policy ("a bug fix that wasn't caught by an
   existing test MUST add a test that would have caught it"), the six invariants (esp. **no
   cross-plugin imports** and **capabilities minimized**), and the shadcn/UI rules (N/A here).
4. Invoke the `security-checklist` skill for F-1 (it touches the `commit-notify` IPC handler,
   which processes runner-supplied bundles across the sandbox→host trust boundary).

---

## F-1 — make re-sync work on the single-replica `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) backend

**Problem.** The re-sync signal currently only fires from the `commit-notify` handler's
**export-baseline-bundle** catch (added in PR #132,
`packages/ipc-core/src/handlers/workspace-commit-notify.ts` ~lines 150-160 — it forwards
`actualParent` + `baselineBundleBytes`). But the single-replica backend
`packages/workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts` **deliberately does not strict-check HEAD on
export** (see the comment ~line 248 — it bundles a stale-but-reachable OID), so on that
backend the version mismatch does NOT surface at export. It surfaces later at **apply-bundle**
(impl.ts raises `code:'parent-mismatch'` at ~lines 793/1027/1047). The handler's **apply-bundle
parent-mismatch catch** returns a **bare** `{accepted:false, reason}` with no
`actualParent`/`baselineBundleBytes` — so the runner treats it as a true veto and rolls back.
Net: on the single-replica backend, the original turn-loss bug is **unfixed**.

**Fix.** In `workspace-commit-notify.ts`, the apply-bundle `parent-mismatch` catch must
forward the same re-sync envelope as the export-baseline-bundle catch:
1. Confirm the apply-bundle `parent-mismatch` `PluginError` carries `cause.actualParent` on
   BOTH backends (`workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts` and `workspace-git-server/src/client/git-engine.ts`
   — the `parentMismatch` helper). If `workspace-git-core` doesn't set `cause.actualParent`,
   add it (it's the freshly-read mirror/storage HEAD).
2. In the catch, when `actualParent` is present, obtain `baselineBundleBytes` **at**
   `actualParent` by calling the `workspace:export-baseline-bundle` service hook with
   `{version: actualParent}` (since `actualParent` IS the current head, the export succeeds
   and returns the bundle at that head — this is exactly what the runner needs to rebase
   onto). Do NOT import a backend directly — go through the hook (invariant: no cross-plugin
   imports).
3. Return `{accepted:false, actualParent, baselineBundleBytes}` (schema-validated via
   `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema`; `baselineBundleBytes` already validates as canonical
   base64). Other apply-bundle errors still behave as before.

The runner side needs **no change** — its existing re-sync loop already consumes this envelope.

**Regression test (must fail pre-fix).** Add an integration test that drives the
`workspace-git-core` (single-replica) backend through the production sequence: materialize →
turn-1 commit-notify (V1) → a concurrent out-of-band `workspace:apply` advancing to V2 (disjoint
path) → turn-2 `commit-notify` with `parent=V1` → assert the response is
`{accepted:false, actualParent:V2, baselineBundleBytes:<non-empty>}` (NOT a bare veto). Mirror
the existing server-backend regression at
`packages/workspace-git-server/src/client/__tests__/concurrent-attachment-regression.test.ts`,
but exercise the apply-bundle path on workspace-git-core. Use REAL components (no mocks).

---

## F-2 — re-sync the runner's final/idle commit too (not just per-turn)

**Problem.** `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts` has the bounded re-sync + retry
loop in the **per-turn** `result` handler (~lines 1006-1095). But the **final commit** site
(~lines 1182-1212 — the post-`result` commit that captures the SDK's delayed jsonl write, per
PR #127) only logs on `!accepted`; it has **no re-sync**. A concurrent writer racing that
final commit drops the final turn's tail — the same bug class, narrower window.

**Fix.** Extract the per-turn bounded re-sync loop into a single shared helper in the runner
(e.g. `commitNotifyWithResync({ client, root, bundleBytes, parentVersion, reason }):
Promise<{ parentVersion: string | null; outcome: 'accepted'|'rolled-back'|'kept' }>`), and call
it at BOTH sites: the per-turn `result` handler AND the final commit site. Preserve all current
behavior exactly (accept → advance baseline + update parentVersion; concurrent-writer envelope
→ `resyncBaselineAndReplay` + bounded retry, `MAX_RESYNC_ATTEMPTS`; true veto → rollback;
network/5xx → keep tree, accumulate; `reb===null` after resync → promote parentVersion). The
helper must keep the result handler thin and must NOT alter `chat:turn-end` emission.

**Regression test (must fail pre-fix).** Extend `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/__tests__/main.test.ts`:
drive a session where the **final** commit-notify (after the turn loop drains) returns the
concurrent-writer envelope, and assert the runner re-syncs + retries (final tail persists)
rather than just logging. Reuse the existing fake-client/queryMock harness from the
`concurrent-writer advance` tests in that file.

---

## Definition of done

- Both F-1 and F-2 implemented, each with a regression test that is RED before the fix.
- `pnpm build` (whole repo) + `pnpm test` (at least the changed packages: `@ax/ipc-core`,
  `@ax/workspace-git-core`, `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`, `@ax/ipc-protocol`; ideally
  whole-repo) green; `pnpm exec eslint <changed files>` clean (scope to changed files — a
  whole-repo `pnpm lint` exits 1 on stale worktree dirs that aren't yours).
- Update `docs/plans/2026-05-23-chat-transcript-loss-fix-impl.md` §Follow-ups to mark F-1/F-2
  done.
- Open a PR (CodeRabbit auto-reviews PRs based on main; it rate-limits, so batch fixes).
- The shared per-(userId,agentId) mirror + concurrent-writer model is the crux — if anything
  about the topology is unclear, re-read the root-cause section of the plan doc and the PR #132
  diff before guessing.

## Reuse, don't reinvent
- `parentMismatch` helper + `cause.actualParent` contract — `workspace-git-server/src/client/git-engine.ts`.
- `BundleBytesSchema` (canonical-base64) + the `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponse` envelope — `packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts`.
- `resyncBaselineAndReplay` + `advanceBaseline`/`rollbackToBaseline`/`commitTurnAndBundle` — `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/git-workspace.ts`.
- The export-baseline-bundle parent-mismatch catch (PR #132) is the template for F-1's apply-bundle catch — `workspace-commit-notify.ts` ~150-160.

content.text: This is a well-specified handoff. Let me follow the yolo-ship phases. First, let me gather context: read the plan doc, check whether PR #132 has merged to main, and read the most relevant memory file.

content.content:
1	# Chat transcript-loss-on-concurrent-write — fix plan
2	
3	> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: use superpowers:executing-plans or
4	> superpowers:subagent-driven-development. Steps use `- [ ]` checkboxes. **Read the Design
5	> Decision section and get sign-off before starting Task 3** — the runner re-sync mechanism
6	> has a fork the reviewer must pick.
7	
8	**Goal:** A chat turn must persist even when a concurrent writer (today: `attachments:commit`)
9	advances the shared per-(userId,agentId) workspace mirror out-of-band, so reloading a
10	session never loses turns and the resume path never injects synthetic turns.
11	
12	**Architecture:** Two coordinated changes. (1) **Host** stops returning a bare 500 on the
13	optimistic-concurrency mismatch and instead returns `accepted:false` carrying the mirror's
14	actual head — the same re-sync contract the `apply-bundle` path already uses
15	(`parent-mismatch` + `cause.actualParent`). (2) **Runner** stops treating that as a transient
16	error: on a parent-mismatch it re-syncs its local baseline to the actual head, replays the
17	current turn's commit on top, and retries `commit-notify` — bounded. This makes chat robust
18	to *any* concurrent writer, not just attachments.
19	
20	**Tech stack:** TypeScript (pnpm workspaces), vitest, git plumbing (`git-engine.ts`,
21	`git-workspace.ts`), zod (`ipc-protocol`).
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Root cause (confirmed — see `docs/plans/2026-05-23-chat-qa-sweep-design.md` revision + memory)
26	
27	1. Chat turns commit normally; runner pins `parentVersion` to its materialize-time baseline
28	   (`main.ts:384`, advanced only on `accepted` at `main.ts:992`).
29	2. An attachment upload calls `attachments:commit` → `workspace:apply` to the **same**
30	   (userId,agentId) mirror (`attachments/src/handlers.ts:198-203`), advancing
31	   `refs/heads/main` **out-of-band** (confirmed: `b6b299c attachments:commit …` is a child of
32	   the runner's pinned `708115f`).
33	3. The runner's next `commit-notify` → `workspace:export-baseline-bundle({version: 708115f})`
34	   → `exportMirrorBundle` sees `head=b6b299c ≠ 708115f` → throws a plain `Error`
35	   (`git-engine.ts:626-629`).
36	4. `workspace-commit-notify.ts:143-146` maps that throw to `internalError()` (HTTP **500**).
37	5. Runner (`main.ts:1002-1010`) treats 500 as transient: keep working tree, **don't advance
38	   `parentVersion`**, accumulate, retry — forever with the same stale version. Nothing ever
39	   persists.
40	6. ⇒ `conversations:get` truncated ⇒ reload shows only the persisted prefix ⇒ SDK resumes
41	   from a transcript ending on a dangling `tool_result` ⇒ injects `Continue from where you
42	   left off.` / `No response requested.`; uncommitted turns are lost.
43	
44	## Design Decision — RESOLVED: Option A (fetch-and-rebase), approved 2026-05-23
45	
46	The runner must rebuild its baseline at the advanced head before it can replay its turn.
47	`materializeWorkspace` clones a bundle into an **empty** dir, so it can't re-clone in place
48	over the live `/permanent`. Two options for how the runner re-acquires the advanced baseline:
49	
50	- **Option A — fetch-and-rebase (recommended).** Host's `accepted:false` response carries the
51	  new baseline **bundle bytes** (it already builds bundles). Runner writes the bundle to a
52	  temp file, `git fetch`es it into `/permanent`, then `git rebase --onto <newBaseline>
53	  <oldBaseline> main` to replay the turn commit on top. Disjoint paths (attachment file vs
54	  jsonl) ⇒ clean rebase. One round-trip, no extra RPC. Cost: the rejected response grows by a
55	  bundle (already sent on the happy path, so symmetric).
56	- **Option B — re-materialize RPC.** Runner calls a new `workspace.re-materialize` action
57	  (or reuses `workspace.materialize`) into a temp dir, then transplants the turn commit. More
58	  moving parts; cleaner separation. Extra RPC + temp-dir transplant logic.
59	
60	Recommendation: **A** — minimal new surface, reuses the bundle the host already produces, and
61	keeps the version handshake in one response. The tasks below assume A; if B is chosen, Task 1's
62	schema field becomes a trigger flag and Task 3 swaps the fetch source.
63	
64	A genuine **conflicting** rebase (concurrent writer touched the *same* path the turn did) is
65	out of scope for this fix — surface it as `accepted:false reason:'rebase-conflict'` and let the
66	turn fail loudly with a clear error rather than silently losing data. Add a follow-up issue.
67	
68	## File structure
69	
70	- `packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts` — add `actualParent` + `baselineBundleBytes` to the
71	  `accepted:false` branch of `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema` (the re-sync envelope).
72	- `packages/workspace-git-server/src/client/git-engine.ts` — make `exportMirrorBundle` /
73	  the export-baseline-bundle path throw the existing `parent-mismatch` PluginError (with
74	  `actualParent`) instead of a plain Error; expose the current head + its bundle.
75	- `packages/ipc-core/src/handlers/workspace-commit-notify.ts` — catch `parent-mismatch` from
76	  `export-baseline-bundle` and return `accepted:false` + `actualParent` + baseline bundle
77	  (mirror the existing apply-bundle `parent-mismatch` handling at lines 257-275).
78	- `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/git-workspace.ts` — add `resyncBaselineAndReplay()`
79	  (fetch new baseline bundle, `rebase --onto`, re-pin baseline).
80	- `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts` — in the `result` handler, on
81	  `accepted:false` with `actualParent`, call `resyncBaselineAndReplay`, update
82	  `parentVersion`, and retry `commit-notify` (bounded loop).
83	- Tests alongside each (`__tests__/`), plus an end-to-end regression in the canary/acceptance
84	  layer for "attachment upload mid-session then reload shows all turns".
85	
86	---
87	
88	## Task 1: Re-sync envelope on the commit-notify response
89	
90	**Files:**
91	- Modify: `packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts` (the `accepted:false` branch ~line 199-201)
92	- Test: `packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/actions.test.ts`
93	
94	- [ ] **Step 1 — failing test.** Assert the schema accepts a rejected response carrying the
95	  re-sync fields and still accepts the bare `{accepted:false, reason}` shape (back-compat).
96	
97	```ts
98	import { WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema } from '../actions.js';
99	
100	it('accepted:false carries optional re-sync envelope', () => {
101	  const resync = WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema.safeParse({
102	    accepted: false, reason: 'parent-mismatch',
103	    actualParent: 'deadbeef', baselineBundleBytes: 'AAAA',
104	  });
105	  expect(resync.success).toBe(true);
106	  // round-trip the new keys so the test goes RED pre-change (unknown keys are
107	  // stripped → these are undefined until the schema is widened).
108	  if (resync.success && resync.data.accepted === false) {
109	    expect(resync.data.actualParent).toBe('deadbeef');
110	    expect(resync.data.baselineBundleBytes).toBe('AAAA');
111	  }
112	  // back-compat: bare rejection still valid
113	  expect(WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema.safeParse(
114	    { accepted: false, reason: 'bundle author verification failed' },
115	  ).success).toBe(true);
116	});
117	```
118	
119	- [ ] **Step 2 — run, expect FAIL** (`actualParent`/`baselineBundleBytes` unknown keys
120	  stripped, so the resync assertion fails to round-trip).
121	  Run: `pnpm --filter @ax/ipc-protocol test -- actions`
122	
123	- [ ] **Step 3 — implement.** Add the optional fields to the `accepted:false` member:
124	
125	```ts
126	z.object({
127	  accepted: z.literal(false),
128	  reason: z.string(),
129	  // Re-sync envelope (optional, present only on a parent-mismatch rejection):
130	  // the storage tier's current head + a baseline bundle at that head, so the
131	  // runner can rebase its turn onto it and retry. Opaque to the runner.
132	  actualParent: z.string().optional(),
133	  baselineBundleBytes: z.string().optional(),
134	}),
135	```
136	
137	- [ ] **Step 4 — run, expect PASS.** `pnpm --filter @ax/ipc-protocol test -- actions`
138	- [ ] **Step 5 — commit.** `git commit -m "feat(ipc): commit-notify re-sync envelope (actualParent+baseline)"`
139	
140	---
141	
142	## Task 2: Host returns accepted:false + re-sync envelope on version mismatch
143	
144	**Files:**
145	- Modify: `packages/workspace-git-server/src/client/git-engine.ts` (`exportMirrorBundle`
146	  ~603-630; the `export-baseline-bundle` engine method ~827)
147	- Modify: `packages/ipc-core/src/handlers/workspace-commit-notify.ts` (the
148	  `export-baseline-bundle` call site, 136-146)
149	- Test: `packages/ipc-core/src/handlers/__tests__/workspace-commit-notify.test.ts`,
150	  `packages/workspace-git-server/src/client/__tests__/*.test.ts`
151	
152	- [ ] **Step 1 — failing test (handler).** With a workspace plugin stub whose
153	  `export-baseline-bundle` throws `PluginError{code:'parent-mismatch', cause:{actualParent}}`,
154	  assert `commit-notify` returns `{accepted:false, actualParent, baselineBundleBytes}` rather
155	  than a 500.
156	
157	```ts
158	it('commit-notify surfaces parent-mismatch as accepted:false + re-sync envelope', async () => {
159	  const bus = makeBusWith({
160	    'workspace:export-baseline-bundle': () => {
161	      throw new PluginError({ code: 'parent-mismatch', plugin: 'x',
162	        message: 'mirror advanced',
163	        cause: { actualParent: 'newhead', baselineBundleBytes: 'AAAA' } });
164	    },
165	  });
166	  const res = await workspaceCommitNotifyHandler(
167	    { parentVersion: 'oldhead', reason: 'turn', bundleBytes: 'AAAA' }, ctx, bus);
168	  expect(res.status).toBe(200);
169	  expect(res.body).toMatchObject({
170	    accepted: false, actualParent: 'newhead', baselineBundleBytes: 'AAAA',
171	  });
172	});
173	```
174	
175	- [ ] **Step 2 — run, expect FAIL** (current handler returns `internalError()` → 500).
176	  Run: `pnpm --filter @ax/ipc-core test -- workspace-commit-notify`
177	
178	- [ ] **Step 3 — implement (engine).** In `exportMirrorBundle` (git-engine.ts:626), throw the
179	  existing `parent-mismatch` helper carrying the head + (for Option A) bundle the *current*
180	  head so the caller can ship it:
181	
182	```ts
183	// git-engine.ts — replace the plain Error at 626-629
184	if (headOid !== oid) {
185	  throw parentMismatch(
186	    `mirror head ${headOid} does not match requested version ${oid}`,
187	    headOid,
188	  );
189	}
190	```
191	
192	  Then in the `export-baseline-bundle` engine method, on `{version}` mismatch, also attach the
193	  current head's bundle bytes to the thrown error's `cause` (so the handler can forward them):
194	  add `cause.baselineBundleBytes = await (bundle current head)`. (Reuse the existing
195	  bundle-current-head path used by `materialize`'s `export-baseline-bundle({})`.)
196	
197	- [ ] **Step 4 — implement (handler).** In `workspace-commit-notify.ts`, wrap the
198	  `export-baseline-bundle` call (136-146) to catch `parent-mismatch` and return the envelope:
199	
200	```ts
201	try {
202	  const out = await bus.call<WorkspaceExportBaselineBundleInput, WorkspaceExportBaselineBundleOutput>(
203	    'workspace:export-baseline-bundle', ctx, { version: parent });
204	  baselineBundleBytes = out.bundleBytes;
205	} catch (err) {
206	  if (err instanceof PluginError && err.code === 'parent-mismatch') {
207	    const cause = err.cause as { actualParent: string | null; baselineBundleBytes?: string };
208	    const body = {
209	      accepted: false as const,
210	      reason: `parent-mismatch: ${err.message}`,
211	      ...(cause.actualParent ? { actualParent: cause.actualParent } : {}),
212	      ...(cause.baselineBundleBytes ? { baselineBundleBytes: cause.baselineBundleBytes } : {}),
213	    };
214	    const checked = WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema.safeParse(body);
215	    if (!checked.success) return internalError();
216	    return { status: 200, body: checked.data };
217	  }
218	  logInternalError(ctx.logger, 'workspace.commit-notify', err);
219	  return internalError();
220	}
221	```
222	
223	- [ ] **Step 5 — run, expect PASS** (both handler + engine suites).
224	  Run: `pnpm --filter @ax/ipc-core test -- workspace-commit-notify && pnpm --filter @ax/workspace-git-server test`
225	- [ ] **Step 6 — commit.** `git commit -m "fix(workspace): commit-notify returns accepted:false+head on mirror advance (was 500)"`
226	
227	---
228	
229	## Task 3: Runner re-sync helper (fetch advanced baseline + rebase the turn)
230	
231	**Resolve the Design Decision first.** Tasks below assume Option A.
232	
233	**Files:**
234	- Modify: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/git-workspace.ts` (add `resyncBaselineAndReplay`)
235	- Test: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/__tests__/git-workspace.test.ts`
236	
237	- [ ] **Step 1 — failing test.** Build a temp `/permanent` with baseline B0 + one turn commit
238	  on top (touching `a.jsonl`). Build a second bundle representing the advanced head B1 =
239	  B0 + a commit touching a *different* path (`att/file.txt`). Call
240	  `resyncBaselineAndReplay({root, baselineBundleBytes: B1bundle, oldBaseline: B0, newBaseline: B1})`.
241	  Assert: `refs/heads/baseline` now points at B1; `main` contains both the attachment file and
242	  the turn's `a.jsonl`; `git log baseline..main` is exactly the replayed turn commit.
243	
244	```ts
245	it('rebases the turn commit onto the advanced baseline (disjoint paths → clean)', async () => {
246	  const { root, B0, B1, b1bundle } = await fixtureAdvancedMirror();
247	  await resyncBaselineAndReplay({ root, baselineBundleBytes: b1bundle, oldBaseline: B0, newBaseline: B1 });
248	  expect(await revParse(root, 'refs/heads/baseline')).toBe(B1);
249	  expect(await fileExists(root, 'att/file.txt')).toBe(true);   // concurrent writer's file
250	  expect(await fileExists(root, 'a.jsonl')).toBe(true);        // our turn's file
251	  const range = await gitLog(root, 'baseline..main');
252	  expect(range.length).toBe(1);
253	});
254	```
255	
256	- [ ] **Step 2 — run, expect FAIL** (`resyncBaselineAndReplay` not defined).
257	  Run: `pnpm --filter @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner test -- git-workspace`
258	
259	- [ ] **Step 3 — implement.** Fetch the advanced baseline into the live repo and rebase:
260	
261	```ts
262	/**
263	 * Recover from a concurrent-writer advance: the storage tier moved its head
264	 * from `oldBaseline` to `newBaseline` while our turn committed on top of
265	 * `oldBaseline`. Fetch the new baseline and replay our turn commit(s) onto it
266	 * so the next `commit-notify` ships `newBaseline..HEAD`.
267	 *
268	 * Throws on a real rebase conflict (concurrent writer touched a path our turn
269	 * also touched) — the caller surfaces that as a loud turn failure.
270	 */
271	export async function resyncBaselineAndReplay(input: {
272	  root: string; baselineBundleBytes: string; oldBaseline: string; newBaseline: string;
273	}): Promise<void> {
274	  const { root, baselineBundleBytes, oldBaseline, newBaseline } = input;
275	  const bundlePath = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `ax-resync-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2,10)}.bundle`);
276	  await fs.writeFile(bundlePath, Buffer.from(baselineBundleBytes, 'base64'));
277	  try {
278	    // Bring the new baseline objects into our repo.
279	    await expectOk(await runGit(['-C', root, 'fetch', bundlePath, 'main']), 'git fetch resync bundle');
280	    // Replay oldBaseline..main onto newBaseline. Disjoint paths → clean.
281	    const rebase = await runGit(['-C', root, 'rebase', '--onto', newBaseline, oldBaseline, 'main']);
282	    if (rebase.code !== 0) {
283	      await runGit(['-C', root, 'rebase', '--abort']); // leave the tree usable
284	      throw new Error(`resync rebase conflict: ${rebase.stderr}`);
285	    }
286	    // Re-pin baseline to the new head.
287	    await expectOk(await runGit(['-C', root, 'update-ref', 'refs/heads/baseline', newBaseline]), 'git update-ref baseline -> newBaseline');
288	  } finally {
289	    await fs.rm(bundlePath, { force: true });
290	  }
291	}
292	```
293	
294	- [ ] **Step 4 — run, expect PASS.** `pnpm --filter @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner test -- git-workspace`
295	- [ ] **Step 5 — commit.** `git commit -m "feat(runner): resyncBaselineAndReplay — rebase turn onto advanced baseline"`
296	
297	---
298	
299	## Task 4: Wire re-sync into the result handler (bounded retry)
300	
301	**Files:**
302	- Modify: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts` (the `result` handler, 980-1011)
303	- Test: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/__tests__/main.test.ts`
304	
305	- [ ] **Step 1 — failing test.** Drive the result handler with a fake IPC client whose first
306	  `workspace.commit-notify` returns `{accepted:false, actualParent, baselineBundleBytes}` and
307	  whose second returns `{accepted:true, version: actualParent2}`. Assert: `resyncBaselineAndReplay`
308	  was called once, the second commit-notify was sent with `parentVersion === actualParent`, and
309	  the final `parentVersion` is the accepted version. (Use the existing main.test.ts harness +
310	  spy seams.)
311	
312	- [ ] **Step 2 — run, expect FAIL** (handler doesn't re-sync; treats accepted:false as a plain
313	  veto → `rollbackToBaseline`). Run: `pnpm --filter @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner test -- main`
314	
315	- [ ] **Step 3 — implement.** Replace the accepted:false branch (currently rollback) so a
316	  parent-mismatch with `actualParent` re-syncs and retries, bounded (e.g. 3 attempts), and only
317	  a *true* veto (no `actualParent`) rolls back:
318	
319	```ts
320	// inside the result try-block, replacing 984-1011's accept/veto handling
321	let attempt = 0;
322	let pv = parentVersion;
323	for (;;) {
324	  const resp = (await client.call('workspace.commit-notify', {
325	    parentVersion: pv, reason: 'turn', bundleBytes: bundleB64,
326	  })) as WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponse;
327	  if (resp.accepted) { parentVersion = resp.version as unknown as string; await advanceBaseline(env.workspaceRoot); break; }
328	  // re-sync path: concurrent writer advanced the mirror
329	  if (resp.actualParent && resp.baselineBundleBytes && attempt < 3) {
330	    attempt++;
331	    try {
332	      await resyncBaselineAndReplay({
333	        root: env.workspaceRoot, baselineBundleBytes: resp.baselineBundleBytes,
334	        oldBaseline: pv as string, newBaseline: resp.actualParent,
335	      });
336	    } catch (e) { process.stderr.write(`runner: resync failed (${String(e)})\n`); break; }
337	    pv = resp.actualParent;
338	    bundleB64 = (await commitTurnAndBundle({ root: env.workspaceRoot, reason: 'turn' })) ?? bundleB64;
339	    continue;
340	  }
341	  // true veto (no actualParent) or retries exhausted → roll back this turn
342	  process.stderr.write(`runner: workspace rejected: ${resp.reason}\n`);
343	  await rollbackToBaseline(env.workspaceRoot);
344	  break;
345	}
346	```
347	
348	  (Note: `bundleB64` and `parentVersion` must be mutable in this scope; today `bundleB64` is a
349	  `const` from `commitTurnAndBundle` — widen to `let` and re-bundle after rebase since the turn
350	  commit OID changes.)
351	
352	- [ ] **Step 4 — run, expect PASS.** `pnpm --filter @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner test -- main`
353	- [ ] **Step 5 — commit.** `git commit -m "fix(runner): re-sync + retry commit-notify on concurrent-writer advance (was stuck loop)"`
354	
355	---
356	
357	## Task 5: End-to-end regression (attachment mid-session, reload shows all turns)
358	
359	**Files:**
360	- Modify/Create: the attachments or chat acceptance/canary test that exercises a real
361	  `attachments:commit` + a subsequent turn (e.g. `packages/attachments/src/__tests__/` or the
362	  channel-web server canary).
363	- Test: as above.
364	
365	- [ ] **Step 1 — failing test.** In-process (testcontainers/git-server stub): materialize a
366	  workspace; commit a chat turn (advances to V1); call `attachments:commit` (advances to V2,
367	  out-of-band); then run a second chat turn's `commit-notify` with `parentVersion=V1`. Assert
368	  the turn is **accepted** (after re-sync) and a `conversations:get`-style read returns *both*
369	  the attachment commit's file and both turns. Before the fix this turn is rejected/lost.
370	
371	- [ ] **Step 2 — run, expect FAIL** (turn lost — reproduces #8).
372	- [ ] **Step 3 — implement:** none beyond Tasks 1-4; this test pins the integrated behavior.
373	- [ ] **Step 4 — run, expect PASS.**
374	- [ ] **Step 5 — commit.** `git commit -m "test(workspace): regression — concurrent attachments:commit no longer drops chat turns"`
375	
376	---
377	
378	## Verification (whole-branch, before PR)
379	
380	- [ ] `pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` (scope lint to changed files per the stale-worktree note).
381	- [ ] Re-walk the original repro on `ax-next-dev` via the `chat-qa-sweep` skill's #8: new chat →
382	  npx turn → attachment turn → reload → assert all turns present, no synthetic `Continue…` turn,
383	  attachment turn intact. (Rebuild the agent image first — the fix is runner-side, so the fast
384	  hostPath loop does NOT cover it; see k8s-acceptance-loop §6 caveat.)
385	
386	## Boundary review
387	
388	No new service-hook signatures; the change is additive fields on an existing IPC response
389	(`WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema`) + an internal runner helper. The new response fields
390	are opaque to the runner (it only feeds `actualParent` back as the next `parent`), matching the
391	existing `apply-bundle` `parent-mismatch`/`actualParent` contract — no backend-specific field
392	leaks. Patch is internal to the bundle wire; no boundary-review-required hook addition.
393	
394	## Out of scope / follow-ups
395	
396	- Genuine rebase conflicts (concurrent writer touched the same path the turn did) →
397	  `accepted:false reason:'rebase-conflict'`, turn fails loudly. File a follow-up for a real
398	  3-way merge or per-path locking if this proves common.
399	- Whether `attachments:commit` *should* share the chat mirror at all (vs a dedicated
400	  attachment store) — deeper design question; this fix makes the shared-mirror case correct
401	  regardless.
402	
403	### Follow-ups surfaced by the final whole-implementation review (2026-05-23)
404	
405	The fix as built is **server-backend-specific** (it's where the bug was observed). Two gaps
406	remain for "robust to ANY concurrent writer" to fully hold — track these:
407	
408	- **F-1 — single-replica `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) backend still drops the
409	  turn.** That backend's `export-baseline-bundle` bundles a stale-but-reachable OID with no
410	  strict-HEAD check (`workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts:238-260`), so it does NOT raise
411	  parent-mismatch on export. The mismatch surfaces later at `apply-bundle`'s CAS, and the
412	  handler's apply-bundle parent-mismatch catch (`workspace-commit-notify.ts:~291`) returns a
413	  **bare** `{accepted:false}` with no `actualParent`/`baselineBundleBytes` — so the runner
414	  treats it as a true veto and rolls back (original bug, unfixed on single-replica). Fix:
415	  forward the re-sync envelope from the apply-bundle catch too (the apply-bundle
416	  `parent-mismatch` PluginError already carries `actualParent`; it additionally needs a
417	  bundle@actualParent). Then the runner's existing re-sync loop handles it for both backends.
418	- **F-2 — the runner's final/idle commit call site has no re-sync.** `main.ts:~1156` (the
419	  post-`result` "final commit" that captures the SDK's late jsonl write, per PR #127) only
420	  logs on `!accepted`; it doesn't re-sync/retry. A concurrent attachment racing that commit
421	  drops the final turn's tail (narrower, same bug class). Fix: extract the bounded re-sync
422	  loop (Task 4) into a shared helper and use it at both the per-turn and final-commit sites.
423	
424	Neither blocks the per-turn server-backend fix (the observed production bug), but both should
425	land before claiming the general "any concurrent writer, any backend" guarantee.
426

2	name: project_transcript_loss_on_reload_keepalive
3	description: chat-qa-sweep found transcript data-loss on reload under warm-runner keepalive — the last completed turn is dropped from conversations:get and resume injects synthetic turns; PR
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 3c3833f9-b6d8-4fa4-8cd7-67eb7050d87d
8	---
9	
10	Found 2026-05-23 by the first real run of the **chat-qa-sweep** skill against kind
11	`ax-next-dev` (fresh image `ax-next/agent:dev` built 10:16Z, **PR #127's
12	`waitForTurnTranscript` confirmed present in the host bundle**). A serious read-path /
13	transcript-commit bug, distinct from the already-fixed [[project_conversations_get_latency_rootcause]]
14	(PR #127) and [[project_turn_end_transcript_read_race]] (PR #125).
15	
16	**Repro (conversation `cnv_6VVzUDWGh5x6Xaho36X-2w`, admin user):**
17	1. New chat, 3 completed turns: (a) plain Q&A, (b) npx command → Bash tool_use + tool_result
18	   + assistant closing text, (c) attachment upload + agent reads it ("platypus") — all
19	   succeeded live in the UI.
20	2. Full page reload, reopen the session → rendered transcript shows only turns (a) and (b),
21	   and (b)'s closing assistant text is gone. The attachment turn (c) is **absent**.
22	3. `GET /api/chat/conversations/:id` confirms server-side: 5 turns, ending at the npx
23	   `tool_result`. No "platypus", no attachment ref — **not a render bug, the data isn't in
24	   the read path.** A warm runner (`ax-sandbox-*`) still held the session.
25	4. Send one more turn → npx closing text backfills, BUT the **attachment turn is permanently
26	   lost**, and the resume path **injects synthetic turns the user never sent**:
27	   `user "Continue from where you left off."` → `assistant "No response requested."`.
28	
29	**ROOT CAUSE (investigated 2026-05-23, host-log + code evidence — supersedes the initial
30	"deferred-commit" guess):** the per-turn `commit-notify` is FAILING, so turns never persist.
31	Host log (`deploy/ax-next-host`) shows repeated `ipc_dispatch_internal_error` on
32	`workspace:export-baseline-bundle` at the exact turn timestamps:
33	`mirror head b6b299c… does not match requested version 708115f… (concurrent writer or
34	stale version)` (thrown at `workspace-git-server/src/client/git-engine.ts:628`,
35	`exportMirrorBundle`). The runner materialized pinning baseline `708115f`
36	(`workspace-materialize.ts:190` → `export-baseline-bundle({})`), but the git-server mirror
37	head was already/became `b6b299c` — a **materialize↔mirror baseline-OID drift** (same class
38	as [[project_materialize_export_baseline_mismatch]], the 2026-05-04 fix).
39	
40	**Two compounding defects:**
41	1. **No recovery from version drift (runner).** `agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:1002-1010`
42	   treats the commit-notify 500 as transient ("keep working tree, don't advance baseline,
43	   accumulate"). `parentVersion` only advances on `accepted` (main.ts:992). So once drifted,
44	   the runner retries the SAME stale parentVersion forever — permanent stuck loop (identical
45	   `b6b299c`/`708115f` pair across every error, 10:40 and 10:45).
46	2. **Wrong error class (host).** `ipc-core/src/handlers/workspace-commit-notify.ts:143-146`
47	   returns `internalError()` (HTTP 500) when `export-baseline-bundle` throws the version
48	   mismatch — so the runner can't distinguish "re-materialize and retry" from a transient
49	   5xx. Contrast the apply-bundle `parent-mismatch` path (line 258) which returns
50	   `accepted:false` with a reason.
51	
52	**Symptom chain:** failed commit-notify ⇒ turns never reach host storage ⇒ `conversations:get`
53	truncated ⇒ reload shows only the persisted prefix ⇒ SDK resumes from a transcript ending on
54	a dangling `tool_result` ⇒ injects synthetic `Continue from where you left off.` /
55	`No response requested.` and the uncommitted turns (npx tail + attachment turn) are lost.
56	
57	**CONFIRMED concurrent writer = the attachments commit path (ground-truth git ancestry):**
58	on the live host mirror cache (`/tmp/ax-ws-mirror-cache-*/ws-3f2c7b719e614aa9-*`), `708115f`
59	IS an ancestor of `b6b299c`, and the advancing commit is `b6b299c attachments:commit
60	712ba584-…` (preceded by normal `… turn` commits up to `708115f`). So answer (a), not (b).
61	Chain: `POST /api/chat/messages` (channel-web `routes-chat.ts:366`) → `attachments:commit`
62	→ attachments plugin (`packages/attachments/src/handlers.ts:198-203`) does `workspace:apply`
63	to the **shared per-(userId,agentId) mirror** (reason `attachments:commit <id>`), advancing
64	`refs/heads/main` **out-of-band from the chat runner**. The runner's pinned `parentVersion`
65	isn't updated, so its next `commit-notify` fails the version check forever. The attachment
66	UPLOAD is the trigger; the attachment TURN (and everything after) is what gets lost.
67	memory-strata consolidation (fires ~3s after every pod reap) was investigated and ruled out
68	(no `workspace:apply`/`commit-notify`; it reads committed memory + writes its own index).
69	
70	**Fix direction (for the design discussion):** primary = make the runner RECOVER from a
71	stale-parentVersion (re-sync/re-materialize baseline to the mirror head + retry the commit)
72	so chat is robust to ANY concurrent writer; supporting = host returns `accepted:false` with
73	the actual head for the export-baseline-bundle version mismatch (re-sync signal) instead of
74	a bare 500 (the apply-bundle path already returns `parent-mismatch` with the head — the
75	export path should match); alternative = attachments don't advance the chat mirror out-of-
76	band (but the agent needs the file in-workspace, so runner-resync is the more robust fix).
77	Failing test: simulate a concurrent `workspace:apply` between materialize and commit-notify;
78	assert the runner re-syncs and the turn persists.
79	
80	**Status:** FIX MERGED as PR #132 (2026-05-23 15:17, merge commit a052285b; branch deleted).
81	Built subagent-driven (Option A) + addressed CodeRabbit (promote parentVersion on empty
82	rebase, base64-validate baselineBundleBytes, plan-doc test examples). Commits
83	e4129ffc→34043403 + d8983f15: (1) ipc-protocol schema gains optional
84	`actualParent`+`baselineBundleBytes` on `accepted:false`; (2) host `commit-notify` returns
85	`accepted:false`+envelope (not 500) on the `export-baseline-bundle` parent-mismatch; (3)
86	runner `resyncBaselineAndReplay` (git fetch advanced baseline + `rebase --onto` turn + re-pin
87	baseline); (4) runner result handler bounded re-sync+retry loop (MAX_RESYNC_ATTEMPTS=3); (5)
88	real-git host-side regression test. Per-package tests green (runner 218, server 497, ipc-core
89	108, ipc-protocol 139); whole-repo build clean; lint clean on changed files. Final whole-impl
90	review = "ready to merge for the reported (server-backend) bug."
91	
92	**Two follow-up gaps surfaced by the final review (the fix is SERVER-BACKEND-specific):**
93	- **F-1:** single-replica `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) backend doesn't raise
94	  parent-mismatch on export (no strict-HEAD check, `impl.ts:238-260`); the mismatch surfaces
95	  at apply-bundle CAS whose handler catch returns a BARE `accepted:false` (no envelope) →
96	  runner rolls back → bug unfixed there. Fix = forward the envelope from the apply-bundle
97	  parent-mismatch catch too.
98	- **F-2:** runner final/idle commit (`main.ts:~1156`, the PR #127 post-`result` site) has no
99	  re-sync — narrower same-class race. Fix = extract the Task-4 loop into a shared helper used
100	  at both call sites.
101	
102	Cluster + repro conversation `cnv_6VVzUDWGh5x6Xaho36X-2w` left intact; port-forward 9090 up.
103	Manual cluster re-walk (rebuild agent image — runner-side change, fast hostPath loop won't
104	cover it) still pending to confirm end-to-end. See [[project_chat_qa_sweep_skill_built]].
105	
106	Also surfaced by the same run (lower priority): npx blocked by `403` from npmjs (agent lacks
107	npm package capability); host pod label is `app.kubernetes.io/name=ax-next-host` (not
108	`…/component=…`); dev-bootstrap is 404 once bootstrapped (auth via
109	[[reference_headless_authed_chat_kind]] cookie-mint). These are skill-accuracy fixes for
110	[[project_chat_qa_sweep_skill_built]].
111
