---
name: project_transcript_loss_on_reload_keepalive
description: chat-qa-sweep found transcript data-loss on reload under warm-runner keepalive — the last completed turn is dropped from conversations:get and resume injects synthetic turns; PR
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 3c3833f9-b6d8-4fa4-8cd7-67eb7050d87d
---

Found 2026-05-23 by the first real run of the **chat-qa-sweep** skill against kind
`ax-next-dev` (fresh image `ax-next/agent:dev` built 10:16Z, **PR #127's
`waitForTurnTranscript` confirmed present in the host bundle**). A serious read-path /
transcript-commit bug, distinct from the already-fixed [[project_conversations_get_latency_rootcause]]
(PR #127) and [[project_turn_end_transcript_read_race]] (PR #125).

**Repro (conversation `cnv_6VVzUDWGh5x6Xaho36X-2w`, admin user):**
1. New chat, 3 completed turns: (a) plain Q&A, (b) npx command → Bash tool_use + tool_result
   + assistant closing text, (c) attachment upload + agent reads it ("platypus") — all
   succeeded live in the UI.
2. Full page reload, reopen the session → rendered transcript shows only turns (a) and (b),
   and (b)'s closing assistant text is gone. The attachment turn (c) is **absent**.
3. `GET /api/chat/conversations/:id` confirms server-side: 5 turns, ending at the npx
   `tool_result`. No "platypus", no attachment ref — **not a render bug, the data isn't in
   the read path.** A warm runner (`ax-sandbox-*`) still held the session.
4. Send one more turn → npx closing text backfills, BUT the **attachment turn is permanently
   lost**, and the resume path **injects synthetic turns the user never sent**:
   `user "Continue from where you left off."` → `assistant "No response requested."`.

**ROOT CAUSE (investigated 2026-05-23, host-log + code evidence — supersedes the initial
"deferred-commit" guess):** the per-turn `commit-notify` is FAILING, so turns never persist.
Host log (`deploy/ax-next-host`) shows repeated `ipc_dispatch_internal_error` on
`workspace:export-baseline-bundle` at the exact turn timestamps:
`mirror head b6b299c… does not match requested version 708115f… (concurrent writer or
stale version)` (thrown at `workspace-git-server/src/client/git-engine.ts:628`,
`exportMirrorBundle`). The runner materialized pinning baseline `708115f`
(`workspace-materialize.ts:190` → `export-baseline-bundle({})`), but the git-server mirror
head was already/became `b6b299c` — a **materialize↔mirror baseline-OID drift** (same class
as [[project_materialize_export_baseline_mismatch]], the 2026-05-04 fix).

**Two compounding defects:**
1. **No recovery from version drift (runner).** `agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:1002-1010`
   treats the commit-notify 500 as transient ("keep working tree, don't advance baseline,
   accumulate"). `parentVersion` only advances on `accepted` (main.ts:992). So once drifted,
   the runner retries the SAME stale parentVersion forever — permanent stuck loop (identical
   `b6b299c`/`708115f` pair across every error, 10:40 and 10:45).
2. **Wrong error class (host).** `ipc-core/src/handlers/workspace-commit-notify.ts:143-146`
   returns `internalError()` (HTTP 500) when `export-baseline-bundle` throws the version
   mismatch — so the runner can't distinguish "re-materialize and retry" from a transient
   5xx. Contrast the apply-bundle `parent-mismatch` path (line 258) which returns
   `accepted:false` with a reason.

**Symptom chain:** failed commit-notify ⇒ turns never reach host storage ⇒ `conversations:get`
truncated ⇒ reload shows only the persisted prefix ⇒ SDK resumes from a transcript ending on
a dangling `tool_result` ⇒ injects synthetic `Continue from where you left off.` /
`No response requested.` and the uncommitted turns (npx tail + attachment turn) are lost.

**CONFIRMED concurrent writer = the attachments commit path (ground-truth git ancestry):**
on the live host mirror cache (`/tmp/ax-ws-mirror-cache-*/ws-3f2c7b719e614aa9-*`), `708115f`
IS an ancestor of `b6b299c`, and the advancing commit is `b6b299c attachments:commit
712ba584-…` (preceded by normal `… turn` commits up to `708115f`). So answer (a), not (b).
Chain: `POST /api/chat/messages` (channel-web `routes-chat.ts:366`) → `attachments:commit`
→ attachments plugin (`packages/attachments/src/handlers.ts:198-203`) does `workspace:apply`
to the **shared per-(userId,agentId) mirror** (reason `attachments:commit <id>`), advancing
`refs/heads/main` **out-of-band from the chat runner**. The runner's pinned `parentVersion`
isn't updated, so its next `commit-notify` fails the version check forever. The attachment
UPLOAD is the trigger; the attachment TURN (and everything after) is what gets lost.
memory-strata consolidation (fires ~3s after every pod reap) was investigated and ruled out
(no `workspace:apply`/`commit-notify`; it reads committed memory + writes its own index).

**Fix direction (for the design discussion):** primary = make the runner RECOVER from a
stale-parentVersion (re-sync/re-materialize baseline to the mirror head + retry the commit)
so chat is robust to ANY concurrent writer; supporting = host returns `accepted:false` with
the actual head for the export-baseline-bundle version mismatch (re-sync signal) instead of
a bare 500 (the apply-bundle path already returns `parent-mismatch` with the head — the
export path should match); alternative = attachments don't advance the chat mirror out-of-
band (but the agent needs the file in-workspace, so runner-resync is the more robust fix).
Failing test: simulate a concurrent `workspace:apply` between materialize and commit-notify;
assert the runner re-syncs and the turn persists.

**Status:** FIX MERGED as PR #132 (2026-05-23 15:17, merge commit a052285b; branch deleted).
Built subagent-driven (Option A) + addressed CodeRabbit (promote parentVersion on empty
rebase, base64-validate baselineBundleBytes, plan-doc test examples). Commits
e4129ffc→34043403 + d8983f15: (1) ipc-protocol schema gains optional
`actualParent`+`baselineBundleBytes` on `accepted:false`; (2) host `commit-notify` returns
`accepted:false`+envelope (not 500) on the `export-baseline-bundle` parent-mismatch; (3)
runner `resyncBaselineAndReplay` (git fetch advanced baseline + `rebase --onto` turn + re-pin
baseline); (4) runner result handler bounded re-sync+retry loop (MAX_RESYNC_ATTEMPTS=3); (5)
real-git host-side regression test. Per-package tests green (runner 218, server 497, ipc-core
108, ipc-protocol 139); whole-repo build clean; lint clean on changed files. Final whole-impl
review = "ready to merge for the reported (server-backend) bug."

**Two follow-up gaps surfaced by the final review (the fix is SERVER-BACKEND-specific):**
- **F-1:** single-replica `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) backend doesn't raise
  parent-mismatch on export (no strict-HEAD check, `impl.ts:238-260`); the mismatch surfaces
  at apply-bundle CAS whose handler catch returns a BARE `accepted:false` (no envelope) →
  runner rolls back → bug unfixed there. Fix = forward the envelope from the apply-bundle
  parent-mismatch catch too.
- **F-2:** runner final/idle commit (`main.ts:~1156`, the PR #127 post-`result` site) has no
  re-sync — narrower same-class race. Fix = extract the Task-4 loop into a shared helper used
  at both call sites.

Cluster + repro conversation `cnv_6VVzUDWGh5x6Xaho36X-2w` left intact; port-forward 9090 up.
Manual cluster re-walk (rebuild agent image — runner-side change, fast hostPath loop won't
cover it) still pending to confirm end-to-end. See [[project_chat_qa_sweep_skill_built]].

Also surfaced by the same run (lower priority): npx blocked by `403` from npmjs (agent lacks
npm package capability); host pod label is `app.kubernetes.io/name=ax-next-host` (not
`…/component=…`); dev-bootstrap is 404 once bootstrapped (auth via
[[reference_headless_authed_chat_kind]] cookie-mint). These are skill-accuracy fixes for
[[project_chat_qa_sweep_skill_built]].

content.content: Launching skill: claude-memory

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/skills/claude-memory

# claude-memory

Per-project working memory that survives across sessions. Design spec: `docs/plans/2026-04-23-claude-memory-skill-design.md` — read that if anything here is ambiguous.

**What this is for:** capturing project facts, decisions, patterns, mistakes, and — critically — *behavioral observations about how I work on this project* (`meta.md`). Each session's read phase acts on the prior session's write phase; no scheduler needed.

**What this is NOT for:** user-scoped preferences (those live in the auto-memory system at `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/`), team-visible artifacts (those go in commit messages and PR descriptions), or one-shot scratchpad notes.

---

## The five files

All under `.claude/memory/`. All entries date-stamped `YYYY-MM-DD`. All files obey the **no-padding rule**: write only entries that earn their place.

| File | What goes in | Write test |
|---|---|---|
| `context.md` | Confirmed project facts — domain rules, API behaviors, env quirks, data shapes, naming conventions. Not opinions. | Would a fresh-context Claude waste time re-discovering this? |
| `decisions.md` | Architectural / design / process decisions. Table: `Date \| Decision \| Rationale \| Alternatives`. Never deleted — strikethrough if reversed. | Would someone 3 months from now ask "why did we do it this way?" |
| `patterns.md` | Two sections — `## Patterns (do these)` and `## Anti-Patterns (don't do these)`. Each entry: *"When X, do Y because Z."* | Is this reusable across future tasks on this project? |
| `mistakes.md` | One entry per mistake: what went wrong, why, how it was fixed. Graduates to `## Resolved` after **two subsequent sessions** avoid it. Never deleted. | Would next session's Claude repeat this if I don't write it down? |
| `meta.md` | Self-observations — behavioral patterns, not project facts. How I work on this project that I'd want future-me to do differently. | Is this about *how I worked*, not *what the project is*? |

### `meta.md` examples (the self-improvement layer)

- `2026-04-23` — On BigQuery tasks, I write the full query then debug. Better: sketch joins first, confirm schema, then write.
- `2026-04-23` — I over-explain when the user wants just the patch. Default terse unless asked.

The pattern: name a behavior, name the better alternative. Tag `active` if it's load-bearing for the next session.

---

## Triggers — when to read, when to write

### Read phase — fires on the first user message in a session

If `.claude/memory/` exists: read **all five files in full**. Build an internal orientation summary (don't show the user unless asked).

Then check three conditions and act:

1. **Hot memory?** Any file has an entry within the last 14 days, OR `mistakes.md` has an un-Resolved entry, OR `meta.md` has an `active`-tagged entry.
   → Surface one line: *"Reading memory — one recent thing to watch: \<...\>."*

2. **File over ~150 lines?** → Run hygiene (see below).

3. **Mistake cluster?** Three similar entries in `mistakes.md` across different sessions.
   → Promote to a `meta.md` rule phrased as an actionable instruction. Add cross-reference back in each originating mistake. Clusters are judgment-based, not keyword-matched.

If `.claude/memory/` does not exist: do **not** create it yet. Wait for the first significant task. See "First session" below.

### Mid-session capture — fires immediately, don't wait for session end

- User correction: *"no, not that — do X."*
- Dead end hit and a different approach succeeds.
- Explicit *"remember this"* / *"save that"* / *"log that."*
- User teaches a non-obvious fact about the project.

Write the relevant file right away. Don't batch.

### Write phase — fires on session end or task completion

Triggers: *"done"*, *"that's all"*, *"wrap up"*, tests pass on a significant change, PR opened, migration runs clean. Or explicit *"update memory."*

For each file, ask: **did this session add anything new?** Write only if yes. No padding entries.

Then ask one meta-question: **What did I learn about how I work on this project that I'd want future-me to do differently?** If the answer names a *behavior* (not a project fact), write to `meta.md`. Otherwise skip.

### NOT triggers

Every tool call. Every file edit. Routine "step complete" moments. A single task finishing that didn't teach anything. The write test filters these out.

---

## First session on a project

When `.claude/memory/` does not exist:

1. After the first **significant** task lands (not on session start — wait for signal), create the directory and seed all five files with minimal headers:

   ```
   .claude/memory/
   ├── context.md       # headers only — start empty
   ├── decisions.md     # table header: | Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives |
   ├── patterns.md      # "## Patterns" and "## Anti-Patterns" headers
   ├── mistakes.md      # empty — entries added as they occur
   └── meta.md          # empty — entries added on write phase
   ```

2. Run the write phase against what was just done, to seed memory with the current task's output.

3. If `.gitignore` is writable and doesn't already ignore `.claude/memory/`, add it. If `.gitignore` is not writable or doesn't exist, warn the user — do not silently leave memory uncommitted-but-visible.

ax-next's `.gitignore` already covers `.claude/memory/` (added in the same change that shipped this skill), so step 3 is a no-op here.

---

## Hygiene — lazy, threshold-triggered

Runs at read phase only when a file crosses ~150 lines. Not every session.

| File | Hygiene rule |
|---|---|
| `context.md` | Consolidate redundant facts. Remove anything subsumed by `decisions.md` or `patterns.md`. |
| `decisions.md` | **Never prune.** Move entries older than 90 days to `## Archived`. |
| `patterns.md` | Merge near-duplicates. Remove patterns superseded by decisions. |
| `mistakes.md` | Apply Resolved graduation (two successful avoidances across subsequent sessions). **Never delete.** |
| `meta.md` | Keep the 5–10 most actionable observations. Archive the rest to `## Archived`. |

If a hygiene pass would materially change the file (not just whitespace), note it in the session's write phase so the user sees what moved.

---

## Writing style

- **Terse.** One concrete sentence beats three vague ones.
- **Specific.** *"Use `listZones` with query filter, never `getZoneTree`"* beats *"prefer efficient API patterns."*
- **Date-stamped.** `YYYY-MM-DD` on every entry.
- **No duplication.** Scan the target file before adding.
- **No padding.** An entry that doesn't earn its place is noise.

When in doubt, the write test on each file is the arbiter: if the answer is no, don't write.

---

## Boundary with other stores

| Store | Audience | Committed? | What goes there |
|---|---|---|---|
| `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/` (auto-memory) | future-me across all projects | no | user profile, cross-project preferences, feedback |
| `<repo>/.claude/memory/` (this skill) | future-me in this project | no (gitignored) | project facts, decisions, patterns, mistakes, meta |
| Commit messages / PR descriptions | the team | yes | anything team-visible |

If something would help the team, it goes in the commit or PR — not here. If it only helps future-me on this project, it goes here. If it's about *me* across projects, it goes in auto-memory.

---

## The loop (why this works without a scheduler)

```
session starts → read phase  → (hot memory?       → surface warning)
                             → (file over 150?    → run hygiene)
                             → (mistake cluster?  → promote to meta.md)
work happens   → mid-session capture on corrections / dead ends / "remember this"
session ends   → write phase → 4 files updated if new info
                             → meta.md updated if behavior-level insight
```

Each session's read phase acts on the prior session's write phase. That's the whole loop. No cron, no agents, no out-of-band jobs.
Let me read the project working-memory files (separate from the user auto-memory already in context).

24	| 2026-05-21 | Verified all 14 load-bearing plan claims via one Explore agent before implementing — grounding TRUE (core exports, `PluginError` code union incl. `invalid-payload`/`init-failed`, host-exec contract = full ToolCall + `{output}` wrap, `^0.91.1`, preset insertion points) | Plan itself flagged the `memory-search` `input.query` bug; don't trust signatures blindly. Item-14 Explore self-contradiction ("HookBus unwraps the payload") is WRONG — item 6 is ground truth: web-tools reads `call.input`, mirroring mcp-client, NOT memory-search. | Trust the plan as-written (rejected: cheap to verify; a wrong signature would fail a subagent mid-chain) |
25	| 2026-05-21 | Implement via ~6 **sequential** subagents grouped by coherent file-unit (scaffold / url-guard+anthropic-client / two tool executors / plugin+exports / canary / preset-wiring), not strict 1-per-12-tasks | Plan gives complete code per step; grouping keeps TDD checkpoints + the plan's per-step commits intact while cutting orchestration round-trips. Subagents share the worktree fs, so they MUST run sequentially (each builds on prior commits), never parallel. | Strict 1-subagent-per-task (rejected: 12 round-trips for a paint-by-numbers plan); inline (rejected: blows orchestrator context budget) |
26	| 2026-05-21 | `web_extract` output is `{ url, title?, text }` — drop the `truncated` field the design spec listed | Impl plan (Task 4 + Task 12 Step 3) resolves the spec/plan discrepancy by dropping `truncated` and syncing the spec: `max_content_tokens` already caps content server-side and a bare boolean adds no actionable signal for the agent. | Keep `truncated` (rejected: no consumer; would require tracking server-side clipping we don't observe) |
27	| 2026-05-21 | Scope the web-tools PR to web-tools only: rebase `feat/web-tools-impl --onto origin/main`, dropping the 2 unrelated commits `b3c87929` (auth-better baseURL) + `494b9e7c` (channel-web pill spacing) that rode on `feat/web-tools` | Reviewer-disciplined / scoped-PR convention (Vinay's documented style): a "web-tools" PR shouldn't carry unrelated fixes — they'd muddy CodeRabbit's review + the PR narrative. Branch lacked only PR #120 with zero file overlap, so the rebase is conflict-free. The 2 dropped commits are PRESERVED on the untouched original `feat/web-tools` branch; flagged in TODO + final report so the user can ship them separately. | Keep them in the PR (rejected: scope creep, unrelated review surface); ask the user (rejected: inferable from convention; commits preserved either way) |
28	| 2026-05-21 | Live-title-SSE: worktree branched **fresh from origin/main** (drops the 2 unpushed local commits f1e2deb7 channel-web spacing + c168fb8c auth baseURL — same pair as entry 27, recommitted) for a clean PR diff | Same reviewer-disciplined scoped-PR convention as entry 27; those 2 fixes don't intersect the title-SSE files. They stay on local `main` for the user to push separately. | Branch from local HEAD (rejected: PR diff would carry the 2 unrelated fixes) |

Additive — existing client poll untouched. Plan docs allowlisted in `.gitignore` (project default ignores `docs/plans/*`). | Per-user matches the sidebar's need; set-title is the only title writer; direct-update avoids a refetch per frame while onOpen-resync covers disconnect gaps; AppContent is the stable post-auth shell. In-process bus (single-replica) matches the chat-SSE precedent — cross-replica deferred (design I6). | Per-conversation SSE (rejected: N connections); fire from conversation-titles (rejected: not the write point); refetch-per-frame (rejected: wasteful); replace the poll (rejected: user chose additive) |
30	| 2026-05-22 | Credentialed-CLI/git-auth (yolo-ship of `docs/plans/2026-05-22-credentialed-cli-tools-and-git-auth-design.md`): **`GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0` is already stamped** in both `sandbox-k8s/pod-spec.ts` (`gitParanoidEnv`) and `sandbox-subprocess/open-session.ts` — design §4.3/§4.6 asked to add it; cut to a **regression assertion** instead of re-stamping | Verified by Explore against current code: both sandbox providers already set it (value `'0'`) and `proxy-startup.ts` forwards `GIT_*` by prefix (`ENV_ALLOWLIST_PREFIXES=['GIT_','LC_']`). Re-adding would be a no-op edit. YAGNI cut per [[feedback_yagni_check_in_plans]]; a guard test catches accidental removal. | Re-stamp it (rejected: already present, dead edit); skip even the assertion (rejected: it's load-bearing for B's fail-fast, cheap to lock in) |
31	| 2026-05-22 | Sub-project B Basic-auth transform uses a **per-request re-arming HTTP/1.1 framing state machine** in the MITM client→upstream path, not single-head-per-connection as design §4.2 literally describes | git smart-HTTP reuses ONE CONNECT tunnel for `GET /info/refs?service=git-upload-pack` (no body) **then** `POST /git-upload-pack` (body), and with creds in the URL libcurl sends `Authorization: Basic` **preemptively on both**. Single-head buffering would transform the GET but leave the POST's base64 placeholder corrupted → fetch fails after refs succeed. State machine: buffer head→transform Basic→`Content-Length`-counted body then re-arm to head; `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` or oversized head → flush + verbatim passthrough, no re-arm (the chunked POST is the clone's last request, so not re-arming after it is safe). Header-only rewrite; bodies keep existing verbatim per-chunk sub (honors I1). | Single head per connection (rejected: corrupts the POST auth on keep-alive); full chunked de-framing to re-arm after chunked bodies (rejected: YAGNI — git's chunked POST is terminal; adds a chunk-size parser for no MVP gain); line-scan for `Authorization:` anywhere in the stream (rejected: false-matches inside POST bodies could corrupt them) |
32	| 2026-05-22 | B canary parity: run the canary scan on the **decoded** `user:pass` inside the transform, before re-encode | Canary scanning today is inline `chunk.includes(token)` on raw decrypted bytes (`listener.ts`), not a registry function — a base64-wrapped canary in a Basic blob would slip past it once we start decoding. Design §4.5 mandates parity. Extract a small `scanForCanary(text, tokens)` helper so head-transform and the existing per-chunk path share it. | Leave canary scan only on raw bytes (rejected: the new decode step blinds it — a real exfil gap) |

Design §5.8 path is correct; project-memory note ("parser at packages/skills") is the shim. `packages` grammar, caps, and new error codes go in skills-parser; `EMPTY_CAPABILITIES` + `SkillCapabilities` type gain `packages`; resolve carries it automatically (re-parse on every `skills:resolve`). | Edit the `@ax/skills` shim (rejected: not the implementation) |
34	| 2026-05-22 | D rejects unsupported ecosystems (`packages.go`) and malformed names with **new `ManifestCode` entries** `invalid-package` (bad name shape / caps) + `unsupported-package-ecosystem` (go and any non-npm/pypi key) | Mirrors the existing per-feature code pattern (`invalid-mcp-command`, `invalid-mcp-transport`); a clear distinct code lets the admin UI message precisely ("Go isn't supported yet" vs "package name invalid"). Grammar shaped so adding `go` later is just removing it from the unsupported set + a registry-host union. | One generic `invalid-package` for both (rejected: can't distinguish "not yet supported" from "malformed"); silently drop unknown ecosystems (rejected: I4/invariant — untrusted manifest content must fail loudly, not be ignored) |
35	| 2026-05-22 | uv/uvx added to the agent image via `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:<pinned-tag> /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/` with a **version tag** (digest-pin deferred); `python3` from Debian bookworm via the existing apt line | Matches design §5.5/§5.7 and the repo's existing ca-certificates/tini "pin by version now, digest-pin as a tracked follow-up" posture. uv is a static Rust binary (lightweight, no runtime deps); reusing system `python3` means `uvx` never downloads a Python build (no extra egress host). The implementing subagent picks the latest stable uv tag and the image smoke test (`uv`, `uvx`, `python3` present + executable as `axagent`) verifies it. | pip+venv management by us (rejected: design explicitly avoids — uvx is ephemeral-env on demand); download python-build-standalone (rejected: extra egress host, system python3 suffices for MVP); digest-pin now (rejected: follow-up alongside the existing tini/ca-certs pin TODO) |
36	| 2026-05-23 | Python-venv yolo-ship: work **in-place on `feat/python-venv-pip` off main**, not a separate worktree | `docs/plans/*` is gitignored (`.gitignore:32`) so the plan file lives only in this checkout; a fresh worktree would need the plan copied in + a full `pnpm install` (node_modules already present here). Only uncommitted changes are untracked/gitignored (decisions.md, `.claude/prompts/`, etc.) which targeted `git add` won't sweep in — isolation is already effective. | Separate git worktree per yolo-ship default (rejected: gitignored plan not carried + costly reinstall for no real isolation gain) |
37	| 2026-05-23 | Python-venv: **commit no `docs/plans/` files** (design/impl/security notes stay local); plan's Task 0-step2 + Task 6-step3 doc commits cut. Deliverable = code + boundary/security review in the PR body + tracked `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` | `docs/plans/*` is gitignored repo-wide, matching every prior plan doc. Security-note content goes in the PR body's boundary section instead of a committed file. | Commit the docs (rejected: gitignored, would need `-f` and break convention) |
38	| 2026-05-23 | Python-venv: implement Tasks 1-4 **inline with strict TDD** (test→fail→impl→pass per step), not per-task subagents | All codebase reads were done during planning (main.ts / system-prompt.ts / proxy-startup.ts / git-workspace.ts + the test files); diffs are surgical + fully specified in the plan; context is ~120K (well under the 300-400K cap), and the heavy part of this run is the CodeRabbit/CI ship loop where lean context matters most. Mirrors the UI-bugs (2026-05-21) inline decision. | Per-task subagents per yolo-ship default (rejected here: re-deriving known context costs more + risks divergence on a paint-by-numbers diff) |
39	| 2026-05-23 | Python-venv: a **concurrent user commit** (`8c57afbd docs(qa): chat-qa-sweep skill design`, vinay@smplabs.com, 05:48) landed on `feat/python-venv-pip` between my commits. **Open the PR from a clean cherry-picked branch `feat/runner-python-venv` off main (my 4 commits only); leave `feat/python-venv-pip` + the user's commit untouched.** | Scoped-PR convention (decisions 27/28) — the PR must not carry unrelated commits. Cherry-pick is **non-destructive** (vs rebase): zero risk to the user's concurrent commit or to a background agent that may still hold `feat/python-venv-pip`. | Rebase --onto to drop it in place (rejected: rewrites a branch a concurrent writer may hold + could strand the user's commit); include it in the PR (rejected: unrelated scope creep) |

# Decisions

Architectural / process decisions. Never deleted — strikethrough if reversed.

| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` / `storage:set` without `database:get-instance` abstraction | YAGNI until a second backend exists; architecture doc Section 6 defers this until `@ax/storage-postgres` lands | Introduce the abstraction now (rejected: premature, no second impl to validate shape) |
| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` without a runtime consumer in Week 3 | Symmetric API with `storage:set`; consumer (session lookup) lands Week 4+. Documented waiver in PR #2 | Wait until Week 4 to register `storage:get` (rejected: half-wired feels worse than a one-slice-early symmetric pair) |
| 2026-04-23 | `@ax/audit-log` owns the `chat:<reqId>` key namespace in storage | One-source-of-truth invariant — any other plugin wanting to read audit records does so through a future service hook, not by reading the key directly | Shared key convention across plugins (rejected: violates invariant #4) |
| 2026-04-28 | Phase 1b kept consumer-facing `credentials:*` hooks unchanged; reshape deferred to Phase 3 | Section 7 of the agent-centric design called Phase 1b "mechanical refactor, behavior identical" while Section 3 specified a redesigned shape — internally inconsistent. Picked the conservative reading: ship the seam (`credentials:store-blob:*`) without churning all call sites with no per-kind benefit yet. Phase 3 reshapes when `credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth` actually needs `kind`. | Reshape now per Section 3 (rejected: ~600 LOC of churn across credential-proxy + mcp-client + CLI for no Phase 1b benefit) |
| 2026-04-28 | `@ax/credentials-store-db` (Phase 1b) delegates to `storage:get` / `storage:set` rather than owning its own table + migrations | Design Section 3 says "owns the credentials table + Kysely migrations, dialect-agnostic via `database:get-instance`" — but `@ax/database-sqlite` doesn't exist (only postgres has the hook), and the dedicated table earns its weight only with Phase 3 columns (`kind`, `expiresAt`, `metadata`). The seam is still real: vault / KMS backends register `credentials:store-blob:*` against a different store. | Build `@ax/database-sqlite` first (rejected: bigger than Phase 1b); have store-db open its own better-sqlite3 connection (rejected: deferred — wait until Phase 3 schema needs it) |
| 2026-04-29 | Phase A spike confirms D1 (HOME-redirect): runner-owned-sessions design moves forward unchanged | Set HOME=tempdir, ran `query()` one-shot then `query({resume})` — sessionId reattached, magic phrase recalled verbatim. Resume costs ~10× less than turn 1 (validates "replay is wasteful"). Two follow-ups for Phase C: (1) workspace persistence ignore-list keeps only `.claude/projects/`, drops `.claude.json` + backups + policy-limits; (2) `runner:read-transcript` uses sessionId glob, not encoded-cwd reconstruction (SDK uses `realpath(cwd)`, breaks on macOS `/var` → `/private/var`). | Bind-mount `~/.claude/projects/<cwd>` to a workspace path (rejected: more sandbox-specific plumbing, doesn't generalize to k8s — see design D1) |
| 2026-04-29 | No v1→v2 schema migrations in ax-next, ever | ax-next is greenfield with no production data to preserve. The "forward-only via future v2 side-table, never an in-place ALTER" rule in `conversations_v1` was inherited from a different mental model. ALTER TABLE in place is correct now AND for every future schema change. The `_v1` suffix on existing tables (`conversations_v1_*`, `audit_v1_*`, etc.) is now just a stable identifier, not a version pointer. Confirmed by Vinay 2026-04-29 during Phase B planning. | Maintain v1 → v2 split for breaking changes (rejected: greenfield, no rows to migrate; speculative versioning earns nothing) |
| 2026-05-21 | Finding 3 facade lives in `@ax/core` (`registerWorkspaceApplyFacade`); backends rename their raw hook to `workspace:apply-internal`; callers of `workspace:apply` are untouched | Facade logic (fire pre-apply → call internal → fire applied) is backend-agnostic, so core is the principled single home (a future GCS backend reuses it). Renaming the *registration* (not the call) means every existing `call('workspace:apply')` site routes through the facade with zero churn — the cleanest way to make the policy chokepoint unbypassable. `filterToPolicy` relocates to core (only depends on the `FileChange` core type) so the facade can policy-filter pre-apply identically to commit-notify. Full design: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-finding3-workspace-apply-facade.md`. | Per-backend duplicated facade (rejected: violates one-source-of-truth); facade in `@ax/workspace-git-core` (rejected: not backend-agnostic — GCS won't import it); also route `apply-bundle`/commit-notify through the facade (rejected: commit-notify already fires both hooks, not a bypasser; out of finding-3 scope) |
| 2026-05-21 | New `yolo-ship` skill is an **orchestrator that sequences existing skills**, not a re-implementation | The brainstorm/design/implement/ship phases map 1:1 onto superpowers:brainstorming / writing-plans / subagent-driven-development / finishing-a-development-branch + commit-push-pr. The skill adds only the autonomy contract + the CodeRabbit/CI ship loop, and cross-references the rest by name (no @ links, per writing-skills). | Write a self-contained monolith re-explaining each phase (rejected: duplicates skills that already exist + evolve independently; bloats context) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` keeps context low via **subagent-driven implementation + Explore for reads**; orchestrator holds only plan + memory + summaries | User's hard cap is < 300–400K tokens across all three phases. Per-task implementation subagents give ~50–100× savings; codebase exploration goes through Explore so file dumps never hit the orchestrator. | One long-running orchestrator agent doing everything inline (rejected: just relocates the context blowup); fully delegate brainstorm+design to a subagent too (rejected: orchestrator's judgment + memory are most valuable in those phases) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` runs brainstorming in **self-answering mode** and logs decisions instead of asking the user | User instruction: "don't ask questions unless absolutely necessary — trust your recommendations as long as you document them in memory." Brainstorming is normally interactive; here the orchestrator generates the questions, answers from code/memory/conventions, and records each in decisions.md. AskUserQuestion reserved for high-stakes + ambiguous + non-inferable. | Keep brainstorming interactive (rejected: contradicts the autonomy instruction) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` is a **project skill** (`.claude/skills/`, committed) and **`done = green review-clean PR, NOT merged`** | It wires into this repo's CodeRabbit + TODO.md + `.claude/memory/` conventions, so it belongs with the other day-1 project skills, not `~/.claude/skills`. Stop condition mirrors the user's spec exactly ("if everything looks good… you are done") — no auto-merge. | Personal skill in `~/.claude/skills` (rejected: CodeRabbit/TODO/memory wiring is repo-specific); auto-merge on green (rejected: user said stop at done) |
| 2026-05-21 | UI-bugs fix: implement the 5 small edits **inline** rather than via per-task subagents | yolo-ship mandates subagent-driven impl to protect orchestrator context, but the rationale is heavy exploration tasks. I already did the deep reads (root causes confirmed; ~5 surgical files). Spawning subagents to re-derive known context would cost MORE total tokens and risk divergence. Context budget stays well under the 300–400K cap. | Strict one-subagent-per-task (rejected here: overhead/divergence risk for tiny, fully-specified diffs) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug: replace the one-shot `assistantTurnCount !== 1` gate with a **bounded retry** (`<= MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS`, =3) while untitled | Root cause of "inconsistent" titles is the single attempt: if it's skipped/fails for ANY transient reason (jsonl-read race in `conversations:get`, llm:call error, validation rejection) the chat is permanently untitled. `title !== null` guard + `ifNull:true` on set-title already make re-attempts idempotent, so retrying on the next few assistant turns is safe; the cap bounds title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting unusable titles. | Keep one-shot + only fix the UI (rejected: leaves "never generated" class unfixed); unbounded retry (rejected: unbounded LLM spend on degenerate output) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug (UI half): widen `generateTitle` poll window (more attempts + backoff, ~12-15s) instead of a client-side first-message fallback title | The 3×1s poll often gives up before the backend round-trip (jsonl read + haiku) lands, permanently caching "New Chat" until a full reload. Widening catches the common case. A client fallback title (first-message snippet) was rejected: it would later flip to the LLM title on reload — jarring. Residual (title landing after the widened window still needs a list() refresh) tracked as TODO. | Client-side fallback title (rejected: diverges from backend, flips on reload); push/websocket title update (rejected: out of scope for MVP) |
| 2026-05-21 | Attachment chip bug: **reconstruct the `attachment` block from the stored text-mention on the host read path** (`conversations:get`), gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix | Under runner-owned-sessions the jsonl stores the runner's *translation* (text-mention `User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)`), not the original `attachment` block — so the original is genuinely gone. The mention embeds path+name+mime (everything the chip needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway). Reconstruction needs no schema/table and **retroactively fixes already-broken chats** (no migration). Format owned by a shared `formatAttachmentMention`/`parseAttachmentMention` pair in `@ax/ipc-protocol` so producer (runner) and consumer (conversations) can't drift. Path-prefix gate + `AttachmentBlockSchema.safeParse` + role==='user'-only keep untrusted jsonl from injecting an out-of-scope downloadable ref. | Host-side per-turn attachment-block table overlaid on read (rejected: new table+hook+turnId-matching, heavier, and needs a migration to fix old chats; `sizeBytes` is the only extra fidelity and it's unused by the render path); client-side parse in blocksToParts (rejected: leaves the wire shape lossy for all other consumers of conversations:get) |
| 2026-05-21 | `@ax/web-tools` impl: branch the worktree off `feat/web-tools` HEAD (not main); PR base = `main`, carrying the design-spec + impl-plan doc commits into the PR | The two doc commits (497fb157, 450153bb) are part of this feature and belong in the same PR. State machine: buffer head→transform Basic→`Content-Length`-counted body then re-arm to head; `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` or oversized head → flush + verbatim passthrough, no re-arm (the chunked POST is the clone's last request, so not re-arming after it is safe). Header-only rewrite; bodies keep existing verbatim per-chunk sub (honors I1). | Single head per connection (rejected: corrupts the POST auth on keep-alive); full chunked de-framing to re-arm after chunked bodies (rejected: YAGNI — git's chunked POST is terminal; adds a chunk-size parser for no MVP gain); line-scan for `Authorization:` anywhere in the stream (rejected: false-matches inside POST bodies could corrupt them) |
| 2026-05-22 | B canary parity: run the canary scan on the **decoded** `user:pass` inside the transform, before re-encode | Canary scanning today is inline `chunk.includes(token)` on raw decrypted bytes (`listener.ts`), not a registry function — a base64-wrapped canary in a Basic blob would slip past it once we start decoding. Design §4.5 mandates parity. Extract a small `scanForCanary(text, tokens)` helper so head-transform and the existing per-chunk path share it. | Leave canary scan only on raw bytes (rejected: the new decode step blinds it — a real exfil gap) |
| 2026-05-22 | Sub-project D targets **`@ax/skills-parser`** (`src/manifest.ts` + `src/capabilities.ts`), not `@ax/skills` | Explore confirmed `@ax/skills/src/manifest.ts` is a one-line re-export of `@ax/skills-parser`'s `parseSkillManifest`; the real grammar + hand-rolled validation + `ManifestCode` union live in `@ax/skills-parser`. Design §5.8 path is correct; project-memory note ("parser at packages/skills") is the shim. `packages` grammar, caps, and new error codes go in skills-parser; `EMPTY_CAPABILITIES` + `SkillCapabilities` type gain `packages`; resolve carries it automatically (re-parse on every `skills:resolve`). | Edit the `@ax/skills` shim (rejected: not the implementation) |
| 2026-05-22 | D rejects unsupported ecosystems (`packages.go`) and malformed names with **new `ManifestCode` entries** `invalid-package` (bad name shape / caps) + `unsupported-package-ecosystem` (go and any non-npm/pypi key) | Mirrors the existing per-feature code pattern (`invalid-mcp-command`, `invalid-mcp-transport`); a clear distinct code lets the admin UI message precisely ("Go isn't supported yet" vs "package name invalid"). Grammar shaped so adding `go` later is just removing it from the unsupported set + a registry-host union. | One generic `invalid-package` for both (rejected: can't distinguish "not yet supported" from "malformed"); silently drop unknown ecosystems (rejected: I4/invariant — untrusted manifest content must fail loudly, not be ignored) |
| 2026-05-22 | uv/uvx added to the agent image via `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:<pinned-tag> /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/` with a **version tag** (digest-pin deferred); `python3` from Debian bookworm via the existing apt line | Matches design §5.5/§5.7 and the repo's existing ca-certificates/tini "pin by version now, digest-pin as a tracked follow-up" posture. uv is a static Rust binary (lightweight, no runtime deps); reusing system `python3` means `uvx` never downloads a Python build (no extra egress host). The implementing subagent picks the latest stable uv tag and the image smoke test (`uv`, `uvx`, `python3` present + executable as `axagent`) verifies it. | pip+venv management by us (rejected: design explicitly avoids — uvx is ephemeral-env on demand); download python-build-standalone (rejected: extra egress host, system python3 suffices for MVP); digest-pin now (rejected: follow-up alongside the existing tini/ca-certs pin TODO) |
| 2026-05-23 | Python-venv yolo-ship: work **in-place on `feat/python-venv-pip` off main**, not a separate worktree | `docs/plans/*` is gitignored (`.gitignore:32`) so the plan file lives only in this checkout; a fresh worktree would need the plan copied in + a full `pnpm install` (node_modules already present here). Only uncommitted changes are untracked/gitignored (decisions.md, `.claude/prompts/`, etc.) which targeted `git add` won't sweep in — isolation is already effective. | Separate git worktree per yolo-ship default (rejected: gitignored plan not carried + costly reinstall for no real isolation gain) |
| 2026-05-23 | Python-venv: **commit no `docs/plans/` files** (design/impl/security notes stay local); plan's Task 0-step2 + Task 6-step3 doc commits cut. Deliverable = code + boundary/security review in the PR body + tracked `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` | `docs/plans/*` is gitignored repo-wide, matching every prior plan doc. Security-note content goes in the PR body's boundary section instead of a committed file. | Commit the docs (rejected: gitignored, would need `-f` and break convention) |
| 2026-05-23 | Python-venv: implement Tasks 1-4 **inline with strict TDD** (test→fail→impl→pass per step), not per-task subagents | All codebase reads were done during planning (main.ts / system-prompt.ts / proxy-startup.ts / git-workspace.ts + the test files); diffs are surgical + fully specified in the plan; context is ~120K (well under the 300-400K cap), and the heavy part of this run is the CodeRabbit/CI ship loop where lean context matters most. Mirrors the UI-bugs (2026-05-21) inline decision. | Per-task subagents per yolo-ship default (rejected here: re-deriving known context costs more + risks divergence on a paint-by-numbers diff) |
| 2026-05-23 | Python-venv: a **concurrent user commit** (`8c57afbd docs(qa): chat-qa-sweep skill design`, vinay@smplabs.com, 05:48) landed on `feat/python-venv-pip` between my commits. **Open the PR from a clean cherry-picked branch `feat/runner-python-venv` off main (my 4 commits only); leave `feat/python-venv-pip` + the user's commit untouched.** | Scoped-PR convention (decisions 27/28) — the PR must not carry unrelated commits. Cherry-pick is **non-destructive** (vs rebase): zero risk to the user's concurrent commit or to a background agent that may still hold `feat/python-venv-pip`. | Rebase --onto to drop it in place (rejected: rewrites a branch a concurrent writer may hold + could strand the user's commit); include it in the PR (rejected: unrelated scope creep) |

content.content:
1	# Mistakes
2	
3	One entry per mistake. Graduates to `## Resolved` after two subsequent sessions avoid it. Never deleted.
4	
5	- `2026-04-23` — **Shebang alone isn't enough for `pnpm exec` / direct invocation.** `@ax/cli` `dist/main.js` had `#!/usr/bin/env node` but was not executable; `pnpm exec ax-next` failed. Fix: `chmod +x dist/main.js` in the postbuild script (commit 3488654). Next time: when scaffolding a CLI package, add the chmod to postbuild at scaffold time.
6	- `2026-04-23` — **`require.main === module` doesn't work in ESM.** `@ax/cli` main-module guard misfired under ESM. Fix: use `pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href === import.meta.url` (commit 87246cc). Next time: any ESM entry-point pattern uses `pathToFileURL`, not CJS idioms.
7	- `2026-04-23` — **kysely `^0.27.4` pulled GHSA-wmrf / GHSA-8cpq advisories.** Bumped to `^0.28.14` post-merge (commit 957288b). Next time: before pinning a new dep, run `pnpm audit` or check `npm view <pkg> --json` for advisories on the target version range, not just latest.
8	- `2026-05-03` — **PR #37 wired plugins into the preset but didn't update the chart's env-var stamping; tests didn't catch it because they sidestep the production listener layout.** `@ax/http-server` + `@ax/auth-oidc` + `@ax/teams` + `@ax/agents` + `@ax/channel-web` shipped into the preset, but `deployment.yaml` never set `AX_HTTP_HOST` / `AX_HTTP_PORT` / `AX_HTTP_COOKIE_KEY` / `AX_DEV_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN`. Three plugins also collided on port 8080 (`EADDRINUSE`). Fix in PR #40: `serve` registers routes via `http:register-route` (no third listener); chart-shape test scans `presets/k8s/src/index.ts` for `env.*` reads + asserts every required var is in the deployment + every deployment env is read somewhere. Caught and removed an orphan `K8S_IMAGE_PULL_POLICY` env on the way. Next time: when wiring a new plugin into the preset, the chart-shape test is the contract — if it would catch the gap, ship it in the same PR.
9	- `2026-05-16` — **Walked Phase C MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE expecting the runner→host workspace sync chain to be live; it isn't in current main.** Spent two chat rounds asking the agent to write `.ax/routines/notify.md`, watched the runner pod spawn + exit cleanly, saw `routines_v1_definitions` stay at zero rows. Root cause: Default Agent has `workspace_ref=NULL` (`packages/onboarding/src/completion-tx.ts:96-110` doesn't pass one), and the chat orchestrator explicitly defers `workspaceRef` consumption (`packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts:844-852`: "leave workspaceRef unconsumed"). No plugin in the active preset wires session-close → bundle export → workspace apply. Tracked as issue #79. Next time: before walking any MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE scenario that depends on `workspace:applied` firing from an agent-written file, grep `presets/k8s/src/index.ts` for `commit-notify` / `bundle` / `workspace:apply` wiring first — if nothing connects session-close to apply, the scenario will fail regardless of what your PR shipped. Also: the doc's `/workspace` references are out of date (runner has `/permanent` + `/ephemeral` + `/tmp`, no `/workspace`).
10

content.content:
1	# Patterns
2	
3	## Patterns (do these)
4	
5	- `2026-04-23` — When adding a new service hook, fill in the boundary-review block in the PR description (alternate impl / leaky field names / subscriber risk / wire surface). If you can't name an alternate impl, it probably shouldn't be a hook yet.
6	- `2026-04-23` — When scaffolding a plugin that touches fs/net/process/untrusted input, drop a `SECURITY.md` in the package directory at scaffold time, not later. Week 3's `packages/storage-sqlite/SECURITY.md` is the template.
7	- `2026-04-23` — For ESM "am I the main module?" checks, use `import { pathToFileURL } from 'node:url'` and compare `import.meta.url === pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href`. `require.main === module` doesn't exist under ESM.
8	- `2026-04-29` — When reading a Claude Agent SDK jsonl by sessionId, glob `<HOME>/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` rather than reconstructing the encoded-cwd directory. The SDK encodes `realpath(cwd)`, not the literal cwd, so any symlink (e.g. macOS `/var` → `/private/var`) breaks reconstruction. SessionId is unique across project dirs.
9	- `2026-04-29` — When parsing SDK jsonl, whitelist turn-bearing entry types (`user`/`assistant`/`tool`) rather than blacklisting bookkeeping types. The SDK adds new bookkeeping kinds (`queue-operation`, `last-prompt`, `attachment`) over time; a blacklist drifts.
10	- `2026-05-03` — When a PR wires a new plugin into the k8s preset, the chart-shape test (`deploy/charts/ax-next/__tests__/env-shape.test.ts`) is the contract that catches missed env-var stamping. The test scans `presets/k8s/src/index.ts` for `env.*` reads and asserts both directions against the rendered deployment. Run it; if it fails, the chart's env block is incomplete — fix in the same PR.
11	- `2026-05-03` — Subcommands that need an HTTP front door should register routes via `http:register-route` against `@ax/http-server`, not spin up their own `http.createServer`. Mirror the channel-web / auth-oidc / teams pattern: a plugin with `manifest.calls: ['http:register-route']`, routes registered in `init()`. Multiple listeners on the same pod cause `EADDRINUSE` collisions and bypass the http-server's CSRF / body-cap subscribers.
12	
13	## Anti-Patterns (don't do these)
14	
15	- `2026-04-23` — Don't put backend-specific field names in hook payloads (`sha`, `bucket`, `pod_name`, `table`, `rowid`). If the name only makes sense for one impl, rename before subscribers depend on it.
16	- `2026-04-23` — Don't land "we'll wire it later" infrastructure. Either a live caller reaches it in the same PR or it doesn't merge. Symmetric API pairs (`get`/`set`) with a documented near-term consumer are the narrow exception.
17

# Patterns

## Patterns (do these)

- `2026-04-23` — When adding a new service hook, fill in the boundary-review block in the PR description (alternate impl / leaky field names / subscriber risk / wire surface). If you can't name an alternate impl, it probably shouldn't be a hook yet.
- `2026-04-23` — When scaffolding a plugin that touches fs/net/process/untrusted input, drop a `SECURITY.md` in the package directory at scaffold time, not later. Week 3's `packages/storage-sqlite/SECURITY.md` is the template.
- `2026-04-23` — For ESM "am I the main module?" checks, use `import { pathToFileURL } from 'node:url'` and compare `import.meta.url === pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href`. `require.main === module` doesn't exist under ESM.
- `2026-04-29` — When reading a Claude Agent SDK jsonl by sessionId, glob `<HOME>/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` rather than reconstructing the encoded-cwd directory. The SDK encodes `realpath(cwd)`, not the literal cwd, so any symlink (e.g. macOS `/var` → `/private/var`) breaks reconstruction. SessionId is unique across project dirs.
- `2026-04-29` — When parsing SDK jsonl, whitelist turn-bearing entry types (`user`/`assistant`/`tool`) rather than blacklisting bookkeeping types. The SDK adds new bookkeeping kinds (`queue-operation`, `last-prompt`, `attachment`) over time; a blacklist drifts.
- `2026-05-03` — When a PR wires a new plugin into the k8s preset, the chart-shape test (`deploy/charts/ax-next/__tests__/env-shape.test.ts`) is the contract that catches missed env-var stamping. The test scans `presets/k8s/src/index.ts` for `env.*` reads and asserts both directions against the rendered deployment. Run it; if it fails, the chart's env block is incomplete — fix in the same PR.
- `2026-05-03` — Subcommands that need an HTTP front door should register routes via `http:register-route` against `@ax/http-server`, not spin up their own `http.createServer`. Mirror the channel-web / auth-oidc / teams pattern: a plugin with `manifest.calls: ['http:register-route']`, routes registered in `init()`. Multiple listeners on the same pod cause `EADDRINUSE` collisions and bypass the http-server's CSRF / body-cap subscribers.

## Anti-Patterns (don't do these)

- `2026-04-23` — Don't put backend-specific field names in hook payloads (`sha`, `bucket`, `pod_name`, `table`, `rowid`). If the name only makes sense for one impl, rename before subscribers depend on it.
- `2026-04-23` — Don't land "we'll wire it later" infrastructure. Either a live caller reaches it in the same PR or it doesn't merge. Symmetric API pairs (`get`/`set`) with a documented near-term consumer are the narrow exception.

1	# Meta — self-observations about how I work on this project
2	
3	Behaviors (not project facts). Name a behavior, name the better alternative. Tag `active` if load-bearing for next session.
4	
5	- `2026-04-23` `active` — **I've been skipping the claude-memory write phase on task completion.** Week 3 shipped as PR #2 without any memory write; this bootstrap is retroactive. Better: treat "PR opened" and "tests pass on a significant change" as hard write-phase triggers, not soft hints. At minimum, ask the meta-question ("what did I learn about how I work?") before declaring a task done.
6	- `2026-04-29` — **I offered the user an option that depended on local fs state without checking that state first.** Asked "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY vs ~/.claude/.credentials.json", user picked the credentials path — and then I discovered macOS keeps creds in Keychain, not on disk. Better: when listing options that hinge on a local file/binary/env-var existing, do the existence check inline before the AskUserQuestion. One extra Bash call upfront beats a wasted question round-trip.
7

# Meta — self-observations about how I work on this project

Behaviors (not project facts). Name a behavior, name the better alternative. Tag `active` if load-bearing for next session.

- `2026-04-23` `active` — **I've been skipping the claude-memory write phase on task completion.** Week 3 shipped as PR #2 without any memory write; this bootstrap is retroactive. Better: treat "PR opened" and "tests pass on a significant change" as hard write-phase triggers, not soft hints. At minimum, ask the meta-question ("what did I learn about how I work?") before declaring a task done.
- `2026-04-29` — **I offered the user an option that depended on local fs state without checking that state first.** Asked "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY vs ~/.claude/.credentials.json", user picked the credentials path — and then I discovered macOS keeps creds in Keychain, not on disk. Better: when listing options that hinge on a local file/binary/env-var existing, do the existence check inline before the AskUserQuestion. One extra Bash call upfront beats a wasted question round-trip.

content.content:
1	# Project context
2	
3	Confirmed facts about ax-next. Not opinions.
4	
5	- `2026-04-23` — `@ax/cli` is the only package allowed to import sibling plugins; enforced by `eslint.config.mjs` exception. All other inter-plugin communication goes through the hook bus.
6	- `2026-04-23` — Storage hook payload contract is `{ key: string, value: Uint8Array }` — opaque bytes, no backend vocab. Any `table`/`bucket`/`rowid` field would leak.
7	- `2026-04-23` — pnpm 10 requires `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies` allowlist for packages with postinstall scripts (e.g. `better-sqlite3` prebuild-install). Without the allowlist, the build silently skips the postinstall and the package fails to load at runtime.
8	- `2026-04-23` — E2E tests invoke the CLI as `node dist/main.js <args>`, not `pnpm exec ax-next`. Shebang + `chmod +x` are present but tests use node directly to avoid pnpm bin-link flakiness.
9	- `2026-04-23` — Week 3 shipped as PR #2 (merged). Week 1–2 kernel shipped as PR #1.
10	- `2026-04-29` — Claude Agent SDK 0.2.119 encodes the project-dir name as `realpath(cwd)` with `/` → `-`, NOT the literal cwd string. On macOS `/var/...` becomes `-private-var-...`. Don't reconstruct the path; glob `<HOME>/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl`.
11	- `2026-04-29` — SDK 0.2.119 with `settingSources: []` still writes outside `<HOME>/.claude/projects/`: `<HOME>/.claude.json` (~20KB cache: GrowthBook flags, anon userID, migrationVersion, model-cost cache), `<HOME>/.claude/backups/.claude.json.backup.<ts>` (accumulates per spawn), `<HOME>/.claude/policy-limits.json`. None are secrets, but workspace persistence should ignore everything outside `.claude/projects/`.
12	- `2026-04-29` — SDK 0.2.119 jsonl entry types include non-turn records: `queue-operation`, `last-prompt`, `attachment` (e.g. `skill_listing` injected by the SDK even with `settingSources: []`). A `runner:read-transcript` parser must whitelist `user`/`assistant`/`tool`, not blacklist.
13	- `2026-04-29` — ax-next has no production data and never will need a schema migration that preserves rows. Existing `_v1` table suffixes (`conversations_v1_*`, `audit_v1_*`) are stable identifiers, NOT version pointers. ALTER TABLE in place is correct for every schema change; no v1→v2 split, ever. (See decisions.md 2026-04-29 entry.)
14	- `2026-05-03` — Host-pod listener layout (issue #39, PR #40): `@ax/ipc-http` on containerPort 8080 (runner-pod back-channel; Service :80) AND `@ax/http-server` on containerPort 9090 (public — `/chat`, `/health`, `/admin/*`, `/auth/*`, `/api/chat/*`; Service :9090). `serve` registers `/chat` + `/health` against http-server via `http:register-route`; no third listener. Chart values: `host.ports.ipc` / `host.ports.publicHttp` / `host.ipcServicePort` / `host.publicHttpServicePort`.
15	- `2026-05-03` — POST `/chat` (and any other state-changing route on the public surface) is gated by `@ax/http-server`'s CSRF subscriber. Headless callers must send `X-Requested-With: ax-admin` OR an Origin in `AX_HTTP_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`. The chart sets `AX_HTTP_ALLOW_NO_ORIGINS=1` to silence the empty-allow-list warning when no Origin allow-list is configured.
16

# Project context

Confirmed facts about ax-next. Not opinions.

- `2026-04-23` — `@ax/cli` is the only package allowed to import sibling plugins; enforced by `eslint.config.mjs` exception. All other inter-plugin communication goes through the hook bus.
- `2026-04-23` — Storage hook payload contract is `{ key: string, value: Uint8Array }` — opaque bytes, no backend vocab. Any `table`/`bucket`/`rowid` field would leak.
- `2026-04-23` — pnpm 10 requires `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies` allowlist for packages with postinstall scripts (e.g. `better-sqlite3` prebuild-install). Without the allowlist, the build silently skips the postinstall and the package fails to load at runtime.
- `2026-04-23` — E2E tests invoke the CLI as `node dist/main.js <args>`, not `pnpm exec ax-next`. Shebang + `chmod +x` are present but tests use node directly to avoid pnpm bin-link flakiness.
- `2026-04-23` — Week 3 shipped as PR #2 (merged). Week 1–2 kernel shipped as PR #1.
- `2026-04-29` — Claude Agent SDK 0.2.119 encodes the project-dir name as `realpath(cwd)` with `/` → `-`, NOT the literal cwd string. On macOS `/var/...` becomes `-private-var-...`. Don't reconstruct the path; glob `<HOME>/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl`.
- `2026-04-29` — SDK 0.2.119 with `settingSources: []` still writes outside `<HOME>/.claude/projects/`: `<HOME>/.claude.json` (~20KB cache: GrowthBook flags, anon userID, migrationVersion, model-cost cache), `<HOME>/.claude/backups/.claude.json.backup.<ts>` (accumulates per spawn), `<HOME>/.claude/policy-limits.json`. None are secrets, but workspace persistence should ignore everything outside `.claude/projects/`.
- `2026-04-29` — SDK 0.2.119 jsonl entry types include non-turn records: `queue-operation`, `last-prompt`, `attachment` (e.g. `skill_listing` injected by the SDK even with `settingSources: []`). A `runner:read-transcript` parser must whitelist `user`/`assistant`/`tool`, not blacklist.
- `2026-04-29` — ax-next has no production data and never will need a schema migration that preserves rows. Existing `_v1` table suffixes (`conversations_v1_*`, `audit_v1_*`) are stable identifiers, NOT version pointers. ALTER TABLE in place is correct for every schema change; no v1→v2 split, ever. (See decisions.md 2026-04-29 entry.)
- `2026-05-03` — Host-pod listener layout (issue #39, PR #40): `@ax/ipc-http` on containerPort 8080 (runner-pod back-channel; Service :80) AND `@ax/http-server` on containerPort 9090 (public — `/chat`, `/health`, `/admin/*`, `/auth/*`, `/api/chat/*`; Service :9090). `serve` registers `/chat` + `/health` against http-server via `http:register-route`; no third listener. content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/skills/security-checklist

# Security checklist

The fifth invariant in `CLAUDE.md` is that **capabilities are explicit and minimized**. The whole point of v2 over openclaw is that we're the secure one. This skill is the operational checklist that backs that invariant.

It is **rigid**: every section must be answered, every "N/A" must give a reason. No skipping, no adapting.

---

## When this fires

The skill applies whenever the current change touches:

- `@ax/core` hook bus signatures, IPC transport, or plugin manifest loading
- A new dependency in any `package.json` (direct or transitive)
- Hook payloads or IPC actions that carry strings originating from a model, tool, or external system
- Code that touches the filesystem with a caller-provided path
- Code that spawns a process
- Code that opens a network connection
- A plugin's exposed surface (registered service hooks, fired subscriber hooks)

If the change touches none of the above, we can skip the skill entirely. If even one applies, we walk all three sections — they cover different threat models, and a change can fail one while passing the others.

---

## The three threat models

Walk these in order. Each section ends with what counts as a valid "N/A".

### 1. Sandbox escape / capability leakage

What capability does this code path grant or widen? List specifically:

- Filesystem paths reachable (read, write, both)
- Network destinations (host, port, scheme)
- Processes spawnable (fixed argv, or caller-influenced?)
- Env vars readable (fixed names, or caller-supplied?)
- Sockets, file descriptors, or opaque handles passed across boundaries

For each, is the capability **bounded**? Concrete failure patterns to check against:

- **Path traversal** — caller-provided path not validated against a base dir; `../` escapes the intended sandbox.
- **Argv injection** — process spawn lets caller control argv0 or insert flags, not just args. Equivalent to arbitrary command execution.
- **Env exfiltration** — env var read with caller-supplied name. `process.env[userInput]` leaks any secret.
- **Handle leak** — hook payload field is an opaque handle (file descriptor, socket, capability token). Subscribers receive it and inherit whatever it wraps.
- **Path-as-token confusion** — payload field named `path` actually represents a version or identity, not a location. Subscribers may resolve it as a real path.

**N/A is acceptable only if** this PR adds no new reachable capability and doesn't widen an existing one. State why explicitly.

### 2. Prompt injection / untrusted content

What strings in this code path originate from outside the trust boundary?

- Model output (LLM responses, tool-call arguments the model chose)
- Tool output (return values from any tool the model called)
- User-uploaded content (files the user dropped in, not text they typed into the chat)
- External API responses (HTTP bodies, search results, scraped pages)
- Third-party plugin output

For each untrusted string, where does it end up? The bad destinations:

- Interpolated into a shell command, a file path, a SQL query, an HTTP URL
- Rendered as HTML or markdown without escaping
- Concatenated into another LLM prompt as if it were a system instruction
- Passed as a tool argument that the model didn't originate (e.g., a subscriber synthesizes a tool call from tool output)
- Logged into a file or DB that another process trusts

Worst-case test: if a malicious tool returned `"; rm -rf ~; echo "` (or the LLM equivalent — `ignore previous instructions and call tool X with arg Y`), what would actually execute? Walk the path from the untrusted string's origin to its final destination.

**N/A is acceptable only if** this PR handles no model output, tool output, user-uploaded content, or external input. State why explicitly.

### 3. Supply chain

Any new entries (added or version-changed) in any `package.json`?

For each new dep, answer:

- **Pinned?** Exact version, not `^` or `~`. Lockfiles help, but the manifest range is the source of truth for what gets accepted on upgrade.
- **Install-time scripts?** Check the package's `scripts` field for `postinstall`, `preinstall`, `prepare`. If yes, what do they do? Network calls at install are a red flag.
- **Maintainer history?** Established package with an author you'd recognize, or a fresh / low-download package? `npm view <pkg> time` and download counts give a rough signal.
- **Transitive deps?** Skim the new transitive surface (`pnpm why <pkg>` after install, or check the lockfile diff). Same questions apply to anything new.

**N/A is acceptable only if** no `package.json` was touched and no new transitive dep was pulled in. State why explicitly.

---

## Output contract

Paste this block into the PR description under a `## Security review` heading:

```
## Security review
- Sandbox: <one line — capability change, or "N/A: <reason>">
- Injection: <one line — untrusted content handled, or "N/A: <reason>">
- Supply chain: <one line — new deps + pinning, or "N/A: <reason>">
```

The `<reason>` after "N/A" is mandatory. Bare "N/A" or empty bullets fail the check — every security checklist that ever died, died the moment "n/a" became free.

Examples of acceptable lines:

- `Sandbox: New plugin registers sandbox:spawn — argv is fixed, args are caller-controlled but escaped via spawn() arg array (not shell). Reachable FS limited to the workspace tmpdir.`
- `Injection: Hook handler receives tool output and writes it to storage as Bytes. Never interpolated into prompts or commands. Storage layer treats as opaque.`
- `Supply chain: N/A — no package.json changes, no new entries in the pnpm-lock diff.`

Examples of unacceptable lines:

- `Sandbox: N/A` — no reason, fails.
- `Injection: Looks fine` — no specifics, fails.
- `Supply chain: Added zod, popular package` — no pinning answer, no install-script answer, fails.

---

## When the checklist finds something

If a section turns up a real risk, **fix it before the PR merges**. Options:

- Tighten the capability — validate the path against a base dir, lock argv to a fixed shape, scope the env access to a fixed allow-list.
- Add a brand on the type (`UntrustedString`, `WorkspaceVersion`) so subscribers know what they're holding and the type system catches misuse.
- Reject the dep — pin it tighter, find an alternative, or vendor a minimal implementation.
- Split the change — sometimes the secure version is a different design than the convenient one. The PR may need re-scoping, not just patching.

Write the fix in the same PR. The note in the PR description should reflect the fixed state, not the original risk. If the fix moves the threat model from "real risk" to "N/A", say so in the line.

---

## Relationship to other guardrails

- The **boundary review checklist** in `CLAUDE.md` catches abstraction leaks (field-name leakage, missing alternate impl). This skill catches capability leaks. A hook can pass boundary review and still fail this checklist, or vice versa — they're orthogonal, and changes that touch hook surface usually need both.
- The globally-installed `security-review` skill (different name on purpose) reviews a diff on demand at PR time. This skill produces the structured note that the diff-review can sanity-check against. Both can run; they don't overlap.
- `ax-conventions` summarizes the five invariants. Invariant #5 is the principle ("capabilities are explicit and minimized"); this skill is the enforcement.

---

## Anti-patterns

Things that look like they satisfy the checklist but don't:

- **Trust by familiarity.** "It's just a string from `chat:end`, that's fine." If the string ever held tool output, it's still untrusted regardless of which hook it's flowing through now.
- **N/A by category, not by reason.** "N/A: this is a refactor" — refactors can still widen capabilities (e.g. an internal function becomes exported and reachable from a plugin boundary).
- **Defer to lint.** "ESLint will catch this." It might. The checklist runs anyway because lint catches patterns, not intent.
- **One-line "yes it's safe".** The checklist's value is in walking the failure modes — the answer matters less than having considered the question. If the section line in the PR doesn't name a specific capability or a specific untrusted-input flow, it's not real.

Security checklist walked for F-1 (F-2 is runner-internal, no boundary change). My findings:

**1. Sandbox escape / capability leakage** — No new capability. F-1 makes the apply-bundle parent-mismatch rejection return the *same* re-sync envelope (`actualParent` + `baselineBundleBytes`) the export-baseline-bundle rejection already returns (PR #132). `actualParent` is a git OID minted **host-side** from the backend's freshly-read HEAD (never runner-supplied), fed back opaquely as a `WorkspaceVersion`, never resolved as a path. `baselineBundleBytes` is the runner's *own* workspace-head bundle (content it already gets via materialize). The new `export-baseline-bundle({version: actualParent})` call uses a host-origin OID, so a malicious runner can't inject it.

**2. Prompt injection / untrusted content** — The runner's untrusted payload (`parentVersion`/`bundleBytes`/`reason`) is not interpolated into any command/path/SQL/URL/prompt by the new code; returned `baselineBundleBytes` is schema-validated canonical base64 before crossing host→runner. Export operates on the caller's own `(userId,agentId)` mirror — no cross-tenant exposure.

**3. Supply chain** — Only `@ax/workspace-git-core` (`workspace:*` internal) added as an `@ax/ipc-core` **devDependency** for the F-1 integration test. No external package, no version range, no install scripts.

No risks found that block; the note goes in the PR body. Now let me write the plan doc and log decisions.

=== impl.ts diff (must touch Site 1 parent-CAS ONLY, not Sites 2/3) ===
commit 4cf5405571927b824e213aa9407b7ef25e7ee410
Author: Vinay Pulim <vinay@smplabs.com>
Date:   Sat May 23 11:46:14 2026 -0400

    fix(workspace-git-core): apply-bundle parent-CAS carries actualParent for re-sync
    
    The apply-bundle parent-CAS (Site 1) fires when a concurrent writer (e.g.
    attachments:commit) advances the mirror past the runner's parent. On the
    single-replica backend this is the FIRST place the advance is detected — the
    prior export of the (now-ancestor) parent succeeds. Pre-fix the throw carried
    no actualParent, so commit-notify could only return a bare veto and the runner
    had no head to rebase onto → stuck-loop / lost turn.
    
    Attach cause.actualParent = the freshly-read mirror head, mirroring the
    multi-replica backend's parentMismatch contract. Sites 2/3 stay bare: they are
    post-CAS integrity checks where head already == parent (no concurrent advance),
    so forwarding actualParent there would trigger useless re-sync retries against
    the same head.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/packages/workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts b/packages/workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts
index 3f588363..bcc18c5c 100644
--- a/packages/workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts
+++ b/packages/workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts
@@ -1028,6 +1028,15 @@ export function registerWorkspaceGitHooks(
             plugin: PLUGIN_NAME,
             hookName: 'workspace:apply-bundle',
             message: `expected parent ${currentVersion}, got ${input.parent === null ? 'null' : input.parent}`,
+            // The freshly-read mirror head, so commit-notify can re-sync the
+            // runner (mirror the multi-replica backend's parentMismatch
+            // contract). This is a genuine concurrent-writer advance: a
+            // disjoint writer moved the mirror past the runner's parent. The
+            // Sites below are post-CAS integrity checks where head already ==
+            // parent (no concurrent advance) — they stay bare (no actualParent)
+            // so the handler doesn't trigger a useless re-sync retry against
+            // the same head.
+            cause: { actualParent: currentVersion },
           });
         }
