=== TODO.md exists? tail ===
-rw-r--r--@ 1 vpulim  staff  12513 May 23 11:23 TODO.md
- [ ] **Cross-agent memory sharing** — explicit user request only (likely requires the Curator pipeline first).
- [ ] **Bring-your-own embedding provider** — Phase 3 dropped vectors; reopen only if Level 7 is re-spiked and lands "IN".

## UI Bugs

- [x] ~~**Errors resume old session and a chat title bug**~~ — fixed (PR #125). BUG 2/3: `channel-web/runtime.tsx` only cleared `conversationRef` on null and never set it on sidebar-select, so selecting an old chat then sending POSTed `conversationId:null` → a new conversation (lost history). Now mirrors `activeSessionId` into the ref unconditionally. BUG 1: single-turn chats stayed "New Chat" because the runner jsonl syncs ~1s after `chat:turn-end`, so `conversations:get` is empty at the only title attempt — `@ax/conversation-titles` now falls back to the turn-end payload's assistant `contentBlocks`.

## Web tools (@ax/web-tools, PR for `feat/web-tools-impl`)

- [x] ~~**Fix the `memory-search` host-executor `call.input` bug.** `packages/memory-strata/src/tools/memory-search.ts` reads `input.query` (bare-input shape) instead of `call.input.query`. The host-execution contract passes the **full `ToolCall`** `{ id, name, input }` to the `tool:execute:<name>` hook (see `@ax/mcp-client` and the new `@ax/web-tools` executors), so `memory-search` reads `undefined` in production — masked only by a unit test that invokes the hook with the wrong shape. Out of scope for the web-tools PR; fix + correct the test together (Bug Fix Policy). Low-risk, ~5-line change.~~ — done (2026-05-22): fixed in `memory-search.ts` **and the two sibling host tools** that shared the identical bug + identical test-masking — `memory-read-section.ts` (`call.input.docId`) and `memory-note.ts` (`call.input.subject`/`content`/…). All three executors now read `call.input`; all three test suites corrected to pass the real `{id,name,input}` envelope via an `asToolCall` helper (verified RED→GREEN per Bug Fix Policy). `pnpm --filter @ax/memory-strata build` + 163 src unit tests + eslint all green.
- [x] ~~**Real-API MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk for web tools.** The new `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` "Web tools" scenario (search → extract → url-guard refusal) is unit/canary-covered only. Real end-to-end needs an org admin to enable Web Search in the Claude Console + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` on the host. Walk it next time the `kind-ax-next-dev` cluster is up with a key.~~ — done (2026-05-22): walked headlessly against `kind-ax-next-dev` (host pod already had `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`; signed the live bootstrap session into `ax_auth_session`, POSTed to `/api/chat/messages`). **web_search** returned live data (Node.js 26.0.0 + dated source URLs → Web Search IS enabled in the Console for this key); **web_extract** returned real nodejs.org/about page content; **url-guard** — the IMDS prompt is independently safe (model self-refuses), and a follow-up `.internal` prompt got the model to actually invoke `web_extract`, exercising the host-side guard: tool returned `invalid-payload` and the host log shows `@ax/web-tools tool:execute:web_extract → "url not allowed (must be a public http(s) URL…)"` at `web-extract.js:32`.
- [x] ~~**Two unrelated fixes parked on `feat/web-tools` (NOT in the web-tools PR).** `b3c87929` (`fix(auth-better): thread AX_PUBLIC_BASE_URL into better-auth baseURL`) and `494b9e7c` (`fix(channel-web): symmetric vertical spacing around tool-group pill`) rode on the `feat/web-tools` branch but are unrelated to web tools, so they were dropped from `feat/web-tools-impl` (rebased `--onto origin/main`) to keep the PR scoped. They are preserved on the untouched `feat/web-tools` branch — ship them via their own PR(s) or cherry-pick.~~ — done (2026-05-21): rebased onto `origin/main` (post-#121) and pushed to `main` as `44087f24` (auth baseURL) + `3d24f597` (channel-web spacing).
- [ ] **Web-tools cost/usage metering + per-agent search-spend visibility.** YAGNI now (plan "Out of scope"). Add a usage-metering subscriber hook if operators need per-agent web-search spend (web search bills ~$10/1k). Trigger: an operator asks for the visibility.

## Credentialed CLI tools & git Basic-auth (PR for `worktree-credentialed-cli-and-git-auth`)

Sub-projects **B** (git Basic-auth substitution in the credential-proxy MITM path) + **D** (skill-declared `capabilities.packages` → registry auto-allowlist + `uv`/`uvx`/`python3` in the agent image). Design: `docs/plans/2026-05-22-credentialed-cli-tools-and-git-auth-design.md`. Shipped green + review-clean; deferred items:

- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (B) — real `git clone` over Basic-auth on kind.** Install a skill declaring `allowedHosts: [gitlab.com]` + a `GITLAB_TOKEN` (`api-key`) credential slot, then have the agent run `git clone https://oauth2:$GITLAB_TOKEN@gitlab.com/<path>.git` and confirm the clone succeeds (the proxy decodes → substitutes → re-encodes the Basic header). Needs `kind-ax-next-dev` up + a real GitLab read token. Covered by unit + MITM integration tests; the cluster walk is the end-to-end proof.
- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (D) — skill-declared CLI through the proxy on kind.** Install a skill declaring `capabilities.packages.npm: ['@linear/cli']` + `allowedHosts: [api.linear.app]` + a `LINEAR_API_KEY` slot, and confirm the agent can `npx @linear/cli ...` (registry auto-allowlisted to `registry.npmjs.org`, tool fetched on demand, Bearer key substituted by the proxy). The agent **image** changed in this PR (D), so rebuild it (`--no-cache` or verify the compiled image per [[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]) before walking.
- [ ] **Digest-pin the agent image's pinned-by-tag deps.** `uv`/`uvx` is pinned to `ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.11.16` (version tag, not digest). Fold a digest-pin into the existing `tini`/`ca-certificates` digest-pin follow-up so all image deps are content-addressed. Low-risk; do them together.
- [ ] **Per-package registry allowlisting (vs whole-registry).** D auto-allowlists the *entire* `registry.npmjs.org` / PyPI when an ecosystem is declared (design §5.7 — bounded by the admin-skill trust boundary + session allowlist + canary, acceptable for MVP). A later tightening could restrict egress to the specific declared package paths. Trigger: a tighter supply-chain posture is requested.
- [ ] **Body-split placeholder substitution across TCP segments (pre-existing, out of scope).** B's `RequestFramer` fixes placeholder substitution for placeholders split across TCP segments in the request **head**; a placeholder split across segments inside a request **body** is still handled only by the per-chunk verbatim path (unchanged from before B). Bearer/api-key creds live in the head, so this rarely bites. Fix only if a real body-credential case appears.
- [ ] **Cross-session CLI tool caching / pre-warming (design §5.6, deferred).** MVP is per-session ephemeral `npx`/`uvx` fetch (re-fetch latency per session). Add a cache/pre-warm step if the latency becomes a problem. YAGNI now.
- [ ] **Go toolchain support (design §2/§5.6, deferred).** `packages.go` is rejected with `unsupported-package-ecosystem`. The grammar is shaped to add it (remove from the unsupported set + union a registry host). Trigger: a skill needs a Go CLI AND the ~300 MB+ image weight is acceptable.

## Concurrent-writer re-sync follow-ups (F-1/F-2, PR for `worktree-f1-f2-resync-followups`)

Completes PR #132 (per-turn server-backend re-sync) so the "robust to ANY concurrent writer, ANY backend" guarantee fully holds. Spec: `.claude/prompts/implement-f1-f2-resync-followups.md` + `docs/plans/2026-05-23-chat-transcript-loss-fix-impl.md` §Follow-ups. Shipped green + review-clean; deferred:

- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE cluster re-walk (F-2, any backend).** Re-walk PR #132's repro on `kind-ax-next-dev`: new chat → npx turn → attachment-upload turn → reload → assert all turns present, no synthetic `Continue…` turn. F-2 is runner-side (the final/idle commit now re-syncs), so the fast hostPath loop won't cover it — **rebuild the agent image first** (per [[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]). Covered by unit + the runner final-commit regression test; the cluster walk is the end-to-end proof.
- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE for F-1 needs a single-replica deployment.** F-1 fixes the `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) single-replica backend's apply-bundle re-sync. The standard `ax-next-dev` cluster runs the multi-replica `@ax/workspace-git-server` backend (where the mismatch surfaces at *export*, the PR #132 path), so it does NOT exercise F-1's apply-bundle catch. Walk F-1 end-to-end only on a deployment configured with the single-replica backend. Covered by the real-backend integration test (`workspace-commit-notify-core-resync.test.ts`).

- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (D) — skill-declared CLI through the proxy on kind.** Install a skill declaring `capabilities.packages.npm: ['@linear/cli']` + `allowedHosts: [api.linear.app]` + a `LINEAR_API_KEY` slot, and confirm the agent can `npx @linear/cli ...` (registry auto-allowlisted to `registry.npmjs.org`, tool fetched on demand, Bearer key substituted by the proxy). The agent **image** changed in this PR (D), so rebuild it (`--no-cache` or verify the compiled image per [[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]) before walking.
- [ ] **Digest-pin the agent image's pinned-by-tag deps.** `uv`/`uvx` is pinned to `ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.11.16` (version tag, not digest). Fold a digest-pin into the existing `tini`/`ca-certificates` digest-pin follow-up so all image deps are content-addressed. Low-risk; do them together.
- [ ] **Per-package registry allowlisting (vs whole-registry).** D auto-allowlists the *entire* `registry.npmjs.org` / PyPI when an ecosystem is declared (design §5.7 — bounded by the admin-skill trust boundary + session allowlist + canary, acceptable for MVP). A later tightening could restrict egress to the specific declared package paths. Trigger: a tighter supply-chain posture is requested.
- [ ] **Body-split placeholder substitution across TCP segments (pre-existing, out of scope).** B's `RequestFramer` fixes placeholder substitution for placeholders split across TCP segments in the request **head**; a placeholder split across segments inside a request **body** is still handled only by the per-chunk verbatim path (unchanged from before B). Bearer/api-key creds live in the head, so this rarely bites. Fix only if a real body-credential case appears.
- [ ] **Cross-session CLI tool caching / pre-warming (design §5.6, deferred).** MVP is per-session ephemeral `npx`/`uvx` fetch (re-fetch latency per session). Add a cache/pre-warm step if the latency becomes a problem. YAGNI now.
- [ ] **Go toolchain support (design §2/§5.6, deferred).** `packages.go` is rejected with `unsupported-package-ecosystem`. The grammar is shaped to add it (remove from the unsupported set + union a registry host). Trigger: a skill needs a Go CLI AND the ~300 MB+ image weight is acceptable.

## Concurrent-writer re-sync follow-ups (F-1/F-2, PR for `worktree-f1-f2-resync-followups`)

Completes PR #132 (per-turn server-backend re-sync) so the "robust to ANY concurrent writer, ANY backend" guarantee fully holds. Spec: `.claude/prompts/implement-f1-f2-resync-followups.md` + `docs/plans/2026-05-23-chat-transcript-loss-fix-impl.md` §Follow-ups. Shipped green + review-clean; deferred:

- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE cluster re-walk (F-2, any backend).** Re-walk PR #132's repro on `kind-ax-next-dev`: new chat → npx turn → attachment-upload turn → reload → assert all turns present, no synthetic `Continue…` turn. F-2 is runner-side (the final/idle commit now re-syncs), so the fast hostPath loop won't cover it — **rebuild the agent image first** (per [[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]). Covered by unit + the runner final-commit regression test; the cluster walk is the end-to-end proof.
- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE for F-1 needs a single-replica deployment.** F-1 fixes the `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) single-replica backend's apply-bundle re-sync. The standard `ax-next-dev` cluster runs the multi-replica `@ax/workspace-git-server` backend (where the mismatch surfaces at *export*, the PR #132 path), so it does NOT exercise F-1's apply-bundle catch. Walk F-1 end-to-end only on a deployment configured with the single-replica backend. Covered by the real-backend integration test (`workspace-commit-notify-core-resync.test.ts`).

input.command:
cd /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/f1-f2-resync-followups
gh pr create --base main --title "fix(workspace): re-sync concurrent-writer advance on single-replica backend + final/idle commit (F-1/F-2)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What this is

Completes **PR #132** (`a052285b`), which fixed the concurrent-writer turn-loss bug for the **per-turn** commit on the **multi-replica** `@ax/workspace-git-server` backend. Two documented follow-ups remained for the "robust to ANY concurrent writer, ANY backend" guarantee to fully hold. Both land here.

Spec: `.claude/prompts/implement-f1-f2-resync-followups.md` + `docs/plans/2026-05-23-chat-transcript-loss-fix-impl.md` §Follow-ups (now marked DONE).

### F-1 — single-replica `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) backend

The re-sync signal previously fired only from the `commit-notify` handler's **export-baseline-bundle** catch (PR #132). But workspace-git-core deliberately does **not** strict-check HEAD on export — it bundles a stale-but-reachable OID — so on that backend the version mismatch never surfaces at export. It surfaces later at **apply-bundle** (`parent-mismatch`), whose handler catch returned a **bare** `{accepted:false, reason}` (no `actualParent`/`baselineBundleBytes`). The runner treated that as a true veto and rolled back → original turn-loss bug, **unfixed on single-replica**.

- `packages/workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts`: the apply-bundle **parent-CAS throw (Site 1 only)** now carries `cause.actualParent` (the freshly-read mirror head), mirroring the multi-replica backend's `parentMismatch` contract. Sites 2/3 stay bare — they're post-CAS integrity checks where `head === parent` already holds (not concurrent advances), so forwarding `actualParent` there would trigger useless re-sync retries against the same head.
- `packages/ipc-core/src/handlers/workspace-commit-notify.ts`: the apply-bundle `parent-mismatch` catch now forwards the same re-sync envelope as the export catch. When the error carries `actualParent` but no bundle (single-replica case), it fetches a baseline bundle **at** `actualParent` via the `workspace:export-baseline-bundle` **service hook** (no direct backend import — invariant #2) and returns `{accepted:false, actualParent, baselineBundleBytes}`. A shared `resyncEnvelopeFromCause` helper de-dups the two catches; a double-race export mismatch forwards the fresher envelope, else falls back to a bare veto.

The **runner side needs no change** — its existing bounded re-sync loop already consumes this envelope.

### F-2 — re-sync the runner's final/idle commit too (not just per-turn)

`main.ts` had the bounded re-sync+retry loop only in the **per-turn** `result` handler. The **final commit** site (the post-`result` commit that captures the SDK's delayed jsonl write, per PR #127) only **logged** on `!accepted` — a concurrent writer racing that final commit dropped the final turn's tail (same bug class, narrower window).

- New module `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/commit-notify-resync.ts` extracts the bounded re-sync loop into `commitNotifyWithResync({ client, root, bundleBytes, parentVersion, reason })`, returning `{ parentVersion, outcome }`. (Separate module — not inside `git-workspace.ts` — so the test `vi.mock('../git-workspace.js')` intercepts the helper's git calls; an intra-module call would bypass the mock.)
- `main.ts` calls it at **both** the per-turn AND the final-commit sites. Per-turn behavior is preserved **exactly** (accept→advance+update parentVersion; concurrent-writer envelope→`resyncBaselineAndReplay`+bounded retry, `MAX_RESYNC_ATTEMPTS=3`; true veto→rollback; network/5xx→keep tree; `reb===null`→promote parentVersion). `chat:turn-end` emission is unchanged.

## Tests (TDD — RED before each fix)

- **F-1:** real-backend integration test `packages/ipc-core/src/handlers/__tests__/workspace-commit-notify-core-resync.test.ts` — drives the **real** `@ax/workspace-git-core` backend + the **real** commit-notify handler through materialize → turn-1 → out-of-band `workspace:apply` (V2) → turn-2 (parent=V1), asserting `{accepted:false, actualParent:V2, baselineBundleBytes:<real git bundle>}` (NOT a bare veto). Pre-fix: `actualParent`/`baselineBundleBytes` were `undefined`.
- **F-2:** helper unit test `commit-notify-resync.test.ts` (7 branches) + a new final-commit regression in `main.test.ts` (the final commit-notify returns the concurrent-writer envelope → asserts the runner re-syncs+retries rather than logging). The existing per-turn "concurrent-writer advance" tests still pass (extraction guard).
- `@ax/workspace-git-core` added as an `@ax/ipc-core` **devDependency** for the F-1 integration test (tests may cross-plugin import; eslint `no-restricted-imports` is off for `__tests__`; no dep cycle).

Whole-repo `pnpm build` (tsc) clean; whole-repo `pnpm test` green (runner 245, ipc-core 109, workspace-git-core 12, all others pass); `eslint` clean on changed files.

## Boundary review

No new/changed service-hook signatures. F-1 adds `cause.actualParent` to an existing `parent-mismatch` `PluginError` (matches the multi-replica backend's existing contract) and forwards the existing `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponse` re-sync envelope from a second catch — opaque to the runner (it only feeds `actualParent` back as the next `parent`), no backend-specific field leak. F-2 is runner-internal. No boundary-review hook addition.

## Security review
- Sandbox: F-1 makes the apply-bundle parent-mismatch rejection return the SAME re-sync envelope (`actualParent` + `baselineBundleBytes`) the export rejection already returns (PR #132) — no new capability. `actualParent` is a git OID minted host-side from the backend's freshly-read HEAD (NOT runner-supplied), fed back opaquely as a `WorkspaceVersion`, never resolved as a path; `baselineBundleBytes` is the runner's own workspace-head bundle (content it already gets via materialize). F-2 is runner-internal (extract a shared helper); no boundary change.
- Injection: the runner's untrusted commit-notify payload is NOT interpolated into any command/path/SQL/URL/prompt by the new code; the new export call uses a host-origin OID, and the returned `baselineBundleBytes` is schema-validated canonical base64 (`WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema`/`BundleBytesSchema`) before crossing host→runner. Export operates on the caller's own `(userId,agentId)` mirror — no cross-tenant exposure.
- Supply chain: only `@ax/workspace-git-core` (`workspace:*` internal) added as an `@ax/ipc-core` devDependency for the F-1 integration test — no external package, no version range, no install scripts. No other `package.json` changes.

input.content:
---
name: project_f1_f2_resync_followups_pr133
description: F-1/F-2 follow-ups to PR #132 — concurrent-writer re-sync now covers the single-replica backend (apply-bundle catch) AND the runner's final/idle commit; PR #133
metadata:
  type: project
---

2026-05-23 yolo-ship of `.claude/prompts/implement-f1-f2-resync-followups.md`. Completes
[[project_transcript_loss_on_reload_keepalive]] (PR #132, which fixed only the **per-turn**
commit on the **multi-replica** `@ax/workspace-git-server` backend). PR **#133**, branch
`worktree-f1-f2-resync-followups` off main @ a052285b, **green + review-clean (1 CodeRabbit
nitpick addressed)**, NOT merged (yolo-ship stops at green PR).

**F-1 — single-replica `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) backend.** That backend's
`export-baseline-bundle` does NOT strict-check HEAD (it bundles a stale-but-reachable OID), so
the version mismatch never surfaces at export (the PR #132 catch). It surfaces later at
**apply-bundle** (`parent-mismatch`), whose handler catch returned a BARE `{accepted:false}` →
runner rolled back → original turn-loss bug unfixed there. Fix (2 prod edits):
- `packages/workspace-git-core/src/impl.ts`: the apply-bundle **parent-CAS throw (Site 1 ONLY)**
  now carries `cause.actualParent = currentVersion` (freshly-read head). Sites 2/3 (baseline-mismatch,
  ancestry) stay BARE — at both, `head === input.parent` already holds (post-CAS integrity checks,
  NOT concurrent advances); forwarding actualParent there would cause useless resync-retries.
- `packages/ipc-core/src/handlers/workspace-commit-notify.ts`: apply-bundle `parent-mismatch` catch
  forwards the re-sync envelope like the export catch. When the error has actualParent but no bundle
  (single-replica), it fetches a bundle@actualParent via the `workspace:export-baseline-bundle`
  **hook** (NO direct backend import — invariant #2). Shared `resyncEnvelopeFromCause` helper de-dups
  both catches; double-race export-mismatch forwards the fresher envelope, else bare-veto fallback.
  Runner side UNCHANGED (its loop already consumes the envelope).

**F-2 — runner's final/idle commit had no re-sync.** `main.ts`'s post-`result` final commit (the
PR #127 site that captures the SDK's late jsonl write) only LOGGED on `!accepted` → a concurrent
writer racing it dropped the final turn's tail. Fix: extracted the per-turn bounded re-sync loop
into **new module** `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/commit-notify-resync.ts`
(`commitNotifyWithResync({client,root,bundleBytes,parentVersion,reason}) → {parentVersion,outcome}`),
called at BOTH the per-turn AND final-commit sites. Behavior preserved exactly. **KEY: the helper is
a SEPARATE module importing git ops from `./git-workspace.js`** — so `main.test.ts`'s
`vi.mock('../git-workspace.js')` intercepts its calls (an intra-module call inside git-workspace.ts
would bypass the mock; cross-module import resolves through it). `chat:turn-end` emission untouched.

**Tests (TDD, RED-before each):** F-1 = real-backend integration test
`ipc-core/.../workspace-commit-notify-core-resync.test.ts` (real `@ax/workspace-git-core` on a
HookBus + real handler; materialize→turn-1→out-of-band `workspace:apply`→turn-2 asserts
`{accepted:false, actualParent:V2, baselineBundleBytes:<real bundle>}`, NOT bare veto). The
apply-bundle catch is ONLY reachable with real bundles flowing the full bundler pipeline — a stub
probe with garbage bundles fails earlier (the existing line-240 handler test proves this), so the
integration test is the only way. `@ax/workspace-git-core` added as an `@ax/ipc-core` **devDep**
(tests may cross-plugin import — eslint `no-restricted-imports` is OFF for `__tests__`; no dep
cycle). F-2 = helper unit test (7 branches) + final-commit regression in `main.test.ts`; existing
per-turn "concurrent-writer advance" tests guard the extraction. Whole-repo build (tsc) + test
green (runner 245, ipc-core 109, workspace-git-core 12); lint clean; CI all green.

**Deferred (TODO.md):** MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE cluster re-walk of F-2 on kind (rebuild agent image —
runner-side); F-1 cluster walk needs a **single-replica** deployment (dev cluster runs multi-replica
workspace-git-server, which doesn't exercise F-1's apply-bundle path).

**Reusable lesson:** to test a runner helper that orchestrates git-workspace ops while keeping the
existing `vi.mock('../git-workspace.js')` effective, put the helper in its OWN module that imports
those ops — never inside git-workspace.ts (ESM intra-module calls bypass module mocks).
